Question:
The main pathophysiological disorder in restrictive cardiomyopathy is:
a.Impaired diastolic ventricular function
b.Impaired ventricular contraction
c.Pericardial constriction
d.Thickened walls of both ventricles
e.Functional tricuspid and mitral insufficiency
Reference: Interna medicina (6. izd., str. XXXVIII, 1693). (2022). Medicinska fakulteta; Buča.
Context: Etiopatogeneza. Osnovna patofiziološka značilnost je motnja v polnitvi prekata, diastolični volumen prekata (desnega in/ali levega) je zmanjšan, sistolična funkcija prekata je normalna ali le malo zmanjšana. English: Etiopathogenesis. The basic pathophysiological characteristic is a disturbance in ventricular filling, the diastolic volume of the ventricle (right and/or left) is reduced, the systolic function of the ventricle is normal or only slightly reduced.

Question:
3. What is true about the third heart sound?
A. It is always a pathological finding
 B. It is a low-frequency sound
 C. We hear it just before the first heart sound
 D. The loudness is not proportional to its clinical significance
 E. It is rarely heard in heart failure
Reference: Interna medicina (6. izd., str. XXXVIII, 1693). (2022). Medicinska fakulteta; Buča.
Context: Tretji ton nastane zaradi vibracij prekatnih sten ob nenadni omejitvi raztezanja v času najhitrejše polnitve prekatov v zgodnji diastoli. Je bolj nizkofrekvenčen in običajno tišji od prvega in drugega tona. Skupaj s prvim in drugim tonom spominja na galop in ga opisujemo kot protodiastolični ali ventrikularni galop. English: The third heart sound arises due to vibrations of the ventricular walls when there is a sudden limitation of expansion during the most rapid filling phase of the ventricles in early diastole. It is lower in frequency and usually quieter than the first and second heart sounds. Together with the first and second sounds, it resembles a gallop and is described as a protodiastolic or ventricular gallop.

Question:
4. What does hemoptysis mean?
A. Coughing up pure blood, more than 250 ml in 24 hours
B. Vomiting blood
C. Coughing up blood-streaked sputum
D. Passing bloody stool
E. Bloody expectoration
Reference: Interna medicina (6. izd., str. XXXVIII, 1693). (2022). Medicinska fakulteta; Buča.
Context:
Opredelitev. Izkašeljaj s primesjo manjše ali večje količine sveže ali že delno hematinizirane krvi imenujemo hemoptiza. Če je kri edina sestavina izmečka in jo bolnik izkašlja več kot 250 ml/24 ur, govorimo o hemoptoi. Po navadi krvavitev izvira iz bronhialne cirkulacije.                       English: 
Definition: Coughing up sputum mixed with a small or large amount of fresh or partially hemolyzed blood is called hemoptysis. If blood is the only component of the sputum and the patient coughs up more than 250 ml in 24 hours, we refer to it as massive hemoptysis (hemoptoe). The bleeding usually originates from the bronchial circulation.
 English: Definition.A vacation with a substance of smaller or larger amounts of fresh or partially hematinized blood is called hemoptysis.If the blood is the only ingredient in the scum and the patient coughs it for more than 250 ml/24 hours, we are talking about hemoptoi.Usually bleeding originates from bronchial circulation.ENGLISH:
Definition: Coughing up sputum mixed with a Small or Large amount of Fresh or partially hemolyzed Blood is called hemoptysis.If blood is the only component of the sputum and the patient coughs up the more than 250 ml and 24 house, we refer to it as massive hemoptysis (hemoptoe).The Bleeding usually originates from the bronchial circulation.

Question:
5. How is orthostatic hypotension defined?
a. If a drop in systolic blood pressure is detected when the patient stands up from a supine position
b. If a drop in both systolic and diastolic blood pressure is detected when the patient stands up from a supine position
c. If the patient experiences syncope or dizziness when standing up from a supine position
d. If blood pressure falls by at least 30/20 mm Hg when the patient stands up from a supine position
e. If blood pressure falls by at least 60/30 mm Hg when the patient stands up from a supine position
Text Answer: If blood pressure falls by at least 30/20 mm Hg when the patient stands up from a supine position
Reference: Interna medicina (6. izd., str. XXXVIII, 1693). (2022). Medicinska fakulteta; Buča.
Context:
O ortostatski hipotenziji govorimo, če je tlak stoje vsaj za 30/20 mmHg nižji v primerjavi z meritvijo v leže. Ugotavljanje takšne razlike tlaka med ležečim in stoječim položajem je posebej pomembno pri bolnikih s sumom na feokromocitom (ki imajo zmanjšan volumen plazme in oslabljenje reflekse simpatičnega živčevja), pri sladkornih bolnikih (prizadetost žilnih živcev) in vseh, ki jemljejo zdravila za zniževanje tlaka. English: we speak of orthostatic hypotension if the blood pressure when standing is at least 30/20 mmHg lower compared to the measurement in the supine position.

Assessing such a difference in pressure between the supine and standing positions is particularly important in patients suspected of pheochromocytoma (who have reduced plasma volume and impaired sympathetic nervous system reflexes), in diabetic patients (vascular neuropathy), and in anyone taking blood pressure–lowering medications.

Question:
6. The sensation of difficult breathing reported by the patient in the medical history is best described by the term:

a. Orthopnea
b. Tachypnea
c. Dyspnea
d. Platypnea
e. Trepopnea
Reference: Interna medicina (6. izd., str. XXXVIII, 1693). (2022). Medicinska fakulteta; Buča.
Context:
Opredelitev. Dispneja je občutek neuspešnega in neprijetnega povečanega napora pri dihanju.

Etiologija. Je pogost simptom pri boleznih dihal ter bolezni srca. Dispneja je tudi simptom slabokrvnosti ali bolezni dihalnih mišic. Pogosta je tudi psihogena dispneja.

English: Definition. Dyspnea is the sensation of an unsuccessful and unpleasantly increased effort during breathing.

Etiology. It is a common symptom in respiratory diseases and heart diseases. Dyspnea is also a symptom of anemia or of diseases of the respiratory muscles. Psychogenic dyspnea is also frequent.

Question:
7. Which laboratory test can we use in clinical practice to confirm heart failure as the cause of exertional dyspnea?
a. Troponin T
b. Troponin I
c. Atrial natriuretic peptide (ANP)
d. Brain (B-type) natriuretic peptide (BNP)
e. Erythropoietin
Reference: Interna medicina (6. izd., str. XXXVIII, 1693). (2022). Medicinska fakulteta; Buča.
Context:
Neposredno lahko srčno popuščanje (tudi akutno) potrdimo le s povišanimi vrednostmi možganskega natriuretičnega peptida (BNP) ali NT-odlomka prekurzorja BNP (NT-proBNP), ki sta pri teh bolnikih značilno povišana (normalne vrednosti so odvisne od spola, starosti in laboratorijske metode).
English: Heart failure (even acute) can be confirmed directly only by elevated levels of brain natriuretic peptide (BNP) or the N-terminal fragment of the BNP precursor (NT-proBNP), both of which are characteristically increased in these patients (normal values depend on sex, age, and the laboratory method).

Question:
8. “Organic murmur” means:
a. Pathologically altered hemodynamics
b. The cause is in another organ
c. The heart is morphologically changed
d. The cause is in artificial material
e. It is not a sign of heart disease
Reference: Interna medicina (6. izd., str. XXXVIII, 1693). (2022). Medicinska fakulteta; Buča.
Context:
Vzroki so lahko morfološke spremembe (t. i. organski šumi) ali bolezensko spremenjena hemodinamika (t. i. funkcionalni šumi). Šume najdemo včasih tudi pri zdravih ljudeh – predvsem pri otrocih, lahko tudi pri starostnikih (t. i. akcidetalni ali naključni šumi).
English: The causes can be morphological changes (so-called organic murmurs) or pathologically altered hemodynamics (so-called functional murmurs). Murmurs are sometimes heard even in healthy individuals—especially in children, and also in the elderly (so-called accidental or incidental murmurs).

Question:
9. An accidental (incidental) murmur is:
a. A consequence of trauma (e.g., a motor vehicle accident with blunt chest injury)
b. Randomly detected in the early phase of heart disease
c. Most common in children
d. Soft and most often diastolic
e. Most often a consequence of aortic stenosis
Reference: Interna medicina (6. izd., str. XXXVIII, 1693). (2022). Medicinska fakulteta; Buča.
Context:
Vzroki so lahko morfološke spremembe (t. i. organski šumi) ali bolezensko spremenjena hemodinamika (t. i. funkcionalni šumi). Šume najdemo včasih tudi pri zdravih ljudeh – predvsem pri otrocih, lahko tudi pri starostnikih (t. i. akcidetalni ali naključni šumi).
English: The causes can be morphological changes (so-called organic murmurs) or pathologically altered hemodynamics (so-called functional murmurs). Murmurs are sometimes heard even in healthy individuals—especially in children, and also in the elderly (so-called accidental or incidental murmurs).

Question:
10. Pulmonary embolism most often arises from a clot originating from:
a. The large veins of the legs
b. The large veins of the arms
c. The calf (tibial) veins
d. The pelvic veins
e. The clot forms in the lungs
Text Answer: The large veins of the legs
Reference: Interna medicina (6. izd., str. XXXVIII, 1693). (2022). Medicinska fakulteta; Buča.
Context:
Opredelitev. Pljučna embolija je zamašitev ene ali več pljučnih arterij. To poglavje obravnava pljučne embolije, ki nastanejo zaradi krvnih strdkov. Ti nastanejo večinoma v venah nog, redkeje rok, ali v medeničnih venah. Krvni tok jih potem odnese v pljučne arterije.
Definition. Pulmonary embolism is the occlusion of one or more pulmonary arteries. This section covers pulmonary embolisms that arise from blood clots. These clots most often form in the veins of the legs, less commonly in the arm veins, or in the pelvic veins. The bloodstream then carries them into the pulmonary arteries. English: Definition.pulmonary embolism is a clogging of one or more pulmonary arteries.This chapter deals with pulmonary embolism resulting from blood clots.These occur mostly in the veins of the legs, less often, or in the pelvic veins.The blood flow then takes them to the pulmonary arteries.
Definition.pulmonary embolism is the occlusion of one or more pulmonary arteries.This section covers pulmonary embolisms that arise from blood clots.These Clots Most Optten Form in the Veins of the Legs, Less Commonly in the Arm Veins, Or in the Pelvic Veins.The Bloodstream then Carries Them Into the Pulmonary Arteries.

Question:
11. A patient suddenly loses consciousness and falls to the ground. After a few seconds, consciousness returns. Such an event is called:
a. Psychogenic coma
b. Opisthotonus
c. Decortication
d. Syncope
e. Asystole
Reference: Interna medicina (6. izd., str. XXXVIII, 1693). (2022). Medicinska fakulteta; Buča.
Context:
Sinkopa je kratkotrajna izguba zavesti zaradi akutno zmanjšanega pretoka krvi v možganih. Nastane lahko nenadoma ali po prodromalnih simptomih (omotica, vrtoglavica, motnje vida, oblijevanje vročine, potenje, navzea).
English: Syncope is a brief loss of consciousness due to an acute reduction of blood flow to the brain. It can occur suddenly or following prodromal symptoms (lightheadedness, dizziness, visual disturbances, a feeling of warmth, sweating, nausea).

Question:
12. Which of the following is characteristic of hypertrophic cardiomyopathy?
a. The walls of the heart muscle are of normal thickness
b. The disease is inherited in an autosomal dominant manner
c. All four cardiac chambers are enlarged
d. The only possible treatment is heart transplantation
e. Patients never experience rhythm disturbances
Text Answer: The disease is inherited in an autosomal dominant manner
Reference: Interna medicina (6. izd., str. XXXVIII, 1693). (2022). Medicinska fakulteta; Buča.
Context:
Etiopatogeneza. Hipertrofična kardiomiopatija se v 60 odstotkih deduje večinoma avtosomno dominantno. Gre za mutacije v genih za sarkomerne proteine. V 5–10 odstotkih gre za genetske motnje, ki vključujejo metabolične in nevromuskularne bolezni, kromosomske nepravilnosti in genetske sindrome.
English: Etiopathogenesis. In about 60 percent of cases, hypertrophic cardiomyopathy is inherited, most often in an autosomal dominant pattern. It is caused by mutations in genes encoding sarcomeric proteins. In 5–10 percent of cases it is due to genetic disorders that include metabolic and neuromuscular diseases, chromosomal abnormalities, and genetic syndromes.

Question:
13. What is cardiac index?
a. Heart rate divided by body surface area
b. Heart rate divided by body-mass index
c. Cardiac output (minute volume) divided by body surface area
d. Cardiac output (minute volume) divided by body mass × 100
e. Cardiac output (minute volume) divided by body-mass index
Text Answer: Cardiac output (minute volume) divided by body surface area
Reference: Interna medicina (6. izd., str. XXXVIII, 1693). (2022). Medicinska fakulteta; Buča.
Context:
Zaradi razlik v telesni masi in višini so vrednosti minutnega volumna srca med posamezniki težko primerljive, zato jih delimo s telesno površino; ta količnik imenujemo srčni indeks:

CI = MV / telesna površina

Pri tem pomeni MV minutni volumen srca [l/min], CI pa srčni indeks [l/min/m²].

Normalne vrednosti srčnega indeksa v mirovanju znašajo od 2,6 do 4,2 l/min/m². S pomočjo Fickove ali termodilucijske metode dobimo tudi podatke, potrebne za izračun pljučnega in sistemskega žilnega upora, ki sta pogosto pomembna za odločitev o operaciji ali presaditvi srca.

English: Because differences in body mass and height make resting cardiac output values difficult to compare between individuals, we divide by body surface area; this ratio is called the cardiac index:

CI = CO / body surface area

Here, CO denotes cardiac output [L/min], and CI the cardiac index [L/min/m²].

Normal resting values for the cardiac index range from 2.6 to 4.2 L/min/m². Using the Fick or thermodilution method, we also obtain data needed to calculate pulmonary and systemic vascular resistance, which is often important in decisions about heart surgery or transplantation.

Question:
14. In atrial flutter, the atrial rate limit is:
a. Below 220/min
b. Above 350/min
c. 220–350/min
d. 120–250/min
e. 280–390/min
Reference: Interna medicina (6. izd., str. XXXVIII, 1693). (2022). Medicinska fakulteta; Buča.
Context:
Diagnoza. Pri tipični in netipični undulaciji je spodnja meja frekvence atrijev 220–250 utripov/min, zgornja pa 320–350 utripov/min. Prevajanje skozi atrioventrikularni vozel je pri tipični undulaciji v večini primerov v razmerju 2 : 1, zato je frekvenca prekatov približno 150 utripov/min.
English: Diagnosis. In typical and atypical atrial flutter the lower limit of the atrial rate is 220–250 beats per minute, and the upper limit is 320–350 beats per minute. Conduction through the atrioventricular node in typical flutter is most often at a 2 : 1 ratio, so the ventricular rate is approximately 150 beats per minute.

Question:
15. The most effective method to terminate ventricular fibrillation is:
a. Cardioversion
b. Defibrillation
c. Medications
d. External cardiac massage (chest compressions)
e. Artificial respiration
Reference: Interna medicina (6. izd., str. XXXVIII, 1693). (2022). Medicinska fakulteta; Buča.
Context:
Prekatna fibrilacija
Elektrokardiografsko vidimo povsem neregularne valove (slika 2.117). V hemodinamičnem pogledu pomeni zastoj srca. Potrebno je takojšnje oživljanje z masažo srca. Bolnika takoj defibriliramo (uporabimo veliko energijo in ne sinhroniziran električni sunek). English: Ventricular fibrillation
On electrocardiography we see completely irregular waves (Fig. 2.117). From a hemodynamic standpoint, this means cardiac arrest. Immediate resuscitation with chest compressions is necessary. The patient must be defibrillated immediately (using high-energy, unsynchronized shocks).

Question:
16. The AV node can generate a heart rate in the range of:
a. 40–60
b. 60–100
c. 20–40
d. 40–80
e. The AV node does not have this capability
Reference: Interna medicina (6. izd., str. XXXVIII, 1693). (2022). Medicinska fakulteta; Buča.
Context: Tako je tipična frekvenca srčnega utripa, ki jo narekuje sinusni vozel, med 60 in 80 utripov na minuto, če izvira utrip iz atrioventrikularne povezave, je frekvenca okoli 40 utripov na minuto, medtem ko je lastna frekvenca srčnega utripa prekatov okoli 30 utripov na minuto. English: Thus, the typical heart rate set by the sinoatrial node is between 60 and 80 beats per minute. If the impulse originates from the atrioventricular junction, the rate is about 40 beats per minute, whereas the intrinsic ventricular rate is around 30 beats per minute.

Question:
17. What is the most common cause of mitral regurgitation in developed countries?
a. Mitral valve prolapse
b. Dilatation of the mitral annulus due to left ventricular failure
c. Ischemia of the papillary muscles
d. Infective endocarditis
e. Rupture of a papillary muscle head
Reference: Interna medicina (6. izd., str. XXXVIII, 1693). (2022). Medicinska fakulteta; Buča.
Context: Najpogostejše bolezni, ki povzročijo insuficienco mitralne zaklopke, so prolaps mitralne zaklopke, revmatska vročina, infekcijski endokarditis, sistemske bolezni veziva, degenerativne spremembe v obroču (kalcinacije), ruptura hord in ishemija ali ruptura papilarnih mišic. English: The most common conditions that cause mitral valve insufficiency are mitral valve prolapse, rheumatic fever, infective endocarditis, systemic connective tissue diseases, degenerative changes of the annulus (calcifications), rupture of the chordae tendineae, and ischemia or rupture of the papillary muscles.

Question:
18. The most common causative agents of infective endocarditis are:
a. Gram-positive cocci
b. Gram-negative bacilli
c. Fungi
d. Cardiotropic viruses
e. Intracellular bacteria
Reference: Interna medicina (6. izd., str. XXXVIII, 1693). (2022). Medicinska fakulteta; Buča.
Context: Etiologija. Mikroorganizmi, ki najpogosteje povzročajo endokarditis, so gram-pozitivni koki (viridans streptokoki, stafilokoki, enterokoki, piogeni streptokoki) in bakterije iz skupine HACEK (Haemophilus, Aggregatibacter, Cardiobacterium, Eikenella, Kingella). English: Etiology. The microorganisms most commonly causing endocarditis are gram-positive cocci (viridans streptococci, staphylococci, enterococci, pyogenic streptococci) and bacteria of the HACEK group (Haemophilus, Aggregatibacter, Cardiobacterium, Eikenella, Kingella).

Question:
19. For pain relief in myocardial infarction, we most commonly use:
a. Acetylsalicylic acid (aspirin) 100 mg
b. Acetylsalicylic acid (aspirin) 500 mg
c. Paracetamol 500 mg
d. Morphine 1 mg/kg body weight
e. Morphine 0.1 mg/kg body weight
Reference: Interna medicina (6. izd., str. XXXVIII, 1693). (2022). Medicinska fakulteta; Buča.
Context:
UKREPI NA TERENU OB SUMU NA NSTE-AKS

Acetilsalicilna kislina 250–500 mg per os ali vensko

Nitroglicerin pršilno pod jezik pri hipertenzivnih bolnikih in bolnikih s srčnim popuščanjem

Morfij za zmanjšanje bolečine do 0,1 mg/kg venski ob antimetiku

Vzdrževanje SaO₂ (pulzna oksimetrija) med 94 % in 98 % z dodatkom kisika po potrebi

Urgentni prevoz v regionalno bolnišnico, ki lahko v vsakem času opravi ultrazvok srca, CT prsnega koša in po potrebi ostale diagnostične preiskave

English: FIELD MEASURES WHEN NSTE-ACS IS SUSPECTED

Aspirin 250–500 mg, orally or intravenously

Sublingual nitroglycerin spray in hypertensive patients and those with heart failure

Morphine for pain relief up to 0.1 mg/kg IV (given with an antiemetic)

Maintain SaO₂ (pulse oximetry) at 94 %–98 %, with supplemental oxygen as needed

Urgent transport to a regional hospital capable of performing echocardiography, chest CT, and other diagnostic tests at any time

Question:
20. Primary cardiac arrest means:
a. Cardiac arrest occurring in the hospital
b. Cardiac arrest occurring in the field (outside the hospital)
c. The cause of the arrest is intrinsic to the heart itself
d. The cardiac arrest is secondary to another cause (e.g., pulmonary embolism, hemorrhage, tension pneumothorax)
e. Cardiac arrest whose cause is ventricular fibrillation
Text Answer: The cause of the arrest is intrinsic to the heart itself
Reference: Interna medicina (6. izd., str. XXXVIII, 1693). (2022). Medicinska fakulteta; Buča.
Context: Pri bolnikih s primarnim zastojem srca sta najpogostejši motnji srčnega ritma prekatna tahikardija brez pulza in prekatna fibrilacija. Prekatna tahikardija je redkejša in pogosto se pri prihodu ekipe nujne pomoči preide v prekatno fibrilacijo. English: In patients with primary cardiac arrest, the most common rhythm disturbances are pulseless ventricular tachycardia and ventricular fibrillation. Ventricular tachycardia is less common and often converts to ventricular fibrillation by the time the emergency medical team arrives.

Question:
21. Kussmaul’s sign is characteristic of:
a. Superior vena cava syndrome
b. Pulmonary embolism
c. Constrictive pericarditis
d. Left-sided heart failure
Reference: Interna medicina (6. izd., str. XXXVIII, 1693). (2022). Medicinska fakulteta; Buča.
Context:
V klinični sliki konstriktivnega perikarditisa vodijo znaki desnostranskega srčnega popuščanja s hepatosplenomegalijo, ascitesom in perifernimi edemi. Zaradi pljučne kongestije se lahko pojavijo tudi dispneja, kašelj in ortopneja. Pri kliničnem pregledu ugotovimo nabrekle vratne vene. Pulzna krivulja vratnih ven je značilna in ima obliko črke W z globokim valom y, ki je posledica hitrega polnjenja prekatov v zgodnji diastoli (Friedreichov znak). Prisotna sta tudi Kussmaulov znak in paradoksni puls, ki pa je manj izrazit kot pri tamponadi. Srčna konica je slabše tipna. English:
In the clinical presentation of constrictive pericarditis, signs of right-sided heart failure prevail, including hepatosplenomegaly, ascites, and peripheral edema. Due to pulmonary congestion, dyspnea, cough, and orthopnea may also occur. On examination, the neck veins are distended. The jugular venous pulse has a characteristic “W” pattern with a deep y-descent, reflecting rapid ventricular filling in early diastole (Friedreich’s sign). Kussmaul’s sign and a paradoxical pulse are also present, though less pronounced than in tamponade. The cardiac apex is poorly palpable.

Question:
22. We auscultate the aortic valve in the:
a. 3rd intercostal space, right parasternal
b. 3rd intercostal space, left parasternal
c. 2nd intercostal space, right parasternal
d. 4th intercostal space, left parasternal
e. 5th intercostal space, left parasternal
Reference: Interna medicina (6. izd., str. XXXVIII, 1693). (2022). Medicinska fakulteta; Buča.
Context: Spremembe na pljučni arteriji včasih zaznamo v drugem medrebrju levo parasternalno, spremembe na aorti pa v drugem medrebrju desno parasternalno. English: Changes in the pulmonary artery are sometimes detected in the second intercostal space at the left parasternal line, whereas changes in the aorta are detected in the second intercostal space at the right parasternal line.

Question:
23. Peripheral arterial disease is:
a. Involvement of small arteries
b. Involvement of arteries distal to the knee
c. A manifestation of atherosclerosis
Reference: Interna medicina (6. izd., str. XXXVIII, 1693). (2022). Medicinska fakulteta; Buča.
Context: Opredelitev. Izraz periferna arterijska bolezen označuje kronične motnje arterijske prekrite vene. V veliki večini primerov jih povzroča ateroskleroza. Najpogosteje je prizadet spodnji ud. Bolezen se razvija počasi in je lahko dokončno asimptomatska. English: Definition. The term peripheral arterial disease refers to chronic disorders of arterial blood supply. In the vast majority of cases, it is caused by atherosclerosis. Most often the lower limb is affected. The disease progresses slowly and can remain completely asymptomatic.

Question:
24. Which of the following conditions is characterized by convergent (narrow‐pulse‐pressure) blood pressure?
a. Severe aortic regurgitation
b. Mitral regurgitation
c. Cardiogenic shock
d. Septic shock
e. Anaphylactic shock
Reference: Interna medicina (6. izd., str. XXXVIII, 1693). (2022). Medicinska fakulteta; Buča.
Context: Konvergentni tlak pomeni majhno razliko med sistoličnim in diastoličnim tlakom in je znak majhnega utripnega volumna in velikega žilnega upora (npr. pri kardiogenem šoku). English: Convergent blood pressure means a small difference between systolic and diastolic pressures and is a sign of a low stroke volume and high vascular resistance (e.g., in cardiogenic shock).

Question:
25. What type of heart murmurs can also be found in healthy people?
a. Quiet, mid‐systolic
b. Quiet, diastolic
c. Quiet, that become louder with exertion
d. Quiet, systolic, that radiate to the neck
e. A heart murmur always means disease
Reference: Interna medicina (6. izd., str. XXXVIII, 1693). (2022). Medicinska fakulteta; Buča.
Context: Največ srčnih šumov je tihih in mezosistoličnih. Če tak šum po naključju odkrijemo pri zdravi osebi, ki ob normalni telesni aktivnosti nima težav, je potreben le natančen klinični pregled, 12-kanalni elektrokardiogram in rentgenogram prsnih organov. Če s tem ne najdemo znakov srčne bolezni, nadaljnje preiskave niso potrebne. English: Most heart murmurs are soft and midsystolic. If such a murmur is discovered incidentally in a healthy person who has no symptoms with normal physical activity, only a thorough clinical examination, a 12-lead electrocardiogram, and a chest X-ray are needed. If these do not reveal any signs of heart disease, no further investigations are necessary.

b. Vomiting blood

Question:
27. Which investigation most easily allows us to determine the cause of acute heart failure?
a. 12-lead electrocardiogram
b. Chest radiograph
c. Echocardiography
d. Chest CT scan
e. Cardiac magnetic resonance imaging
Reference: Interna medicina (6. izd., str. XXXVIII, 1693). (2022). Medicinska fakulteta; Buča.
Context: Transtorakalna ali transezofagealna ultrazvočna preiskava srca. Izmerimo velikost srčnih votlin, debelino prekati, ocenimo celokupno in področ­no krčenje obeh prekatov, diastolično funkcijo levega prekata, morfološke spremembe na zaklopkah in njegove funkcionalne posledice ter morebitni perikardni izliv in spremembe na velikih žilah. Zato je ultrazvočna preiskava ključna diagnostična metoda pri vseh bolnikih z akutnim srčnim popuščanjem, saj z njo pri veliki večini bolnikov ugotovimo vzrok in tudi stopnjo motenega srčnega popuščanja. Opraviti jo moramo čim prej in ob spremembah bolnikovega stanja tudi ponoviti. English: Transthoracic or transesophageal echocardiography. We measure the dimensions of the cardiac chambers, ventricular wall thickness, assess global and segmental contraction of both ventricles, diastolic function of the left ventricle, morphological changes of the valves and their functional consequences, as well as any pericardial effusion and changes in the great vessels. For this reason, echocardiography is the key diagnostic method in all patients with acute heart failure, since it allows us to determine both the cause and the severity of ventricular dysfunction in most patients. It should be performed as soon as possible and repeated if the patient’s condition changes.

Question:
28. When do we say that the cardiac axis on an electrocardiogram is normal?
a. If it is between 30° and 60°
b. If it is between 0° and 90°
c. If it is 45°
d. If it is between –30° and 90°
e. If it is between –60° and 120°
Reference: Interna medicina (6. izd., str. XXXVIII, 1693). (2022). Medicinska fakulteta; Buča.
Context: Normalna srčna os je med –30º in 90º. Med –30º in –90º  je leva os, med 90º in 180º je desna srčna os. Področje med –90º in +180º imenujejo ekstremna leva ali pa nedoločena srčna os. English: The normal cardiac axis is between –30° and 90°. Between –30° and –90° it is called a left axis deviation, and between 90° and 180° a right axis deviation. The region between –90° and +180° is termed an extreme left or indeterminate axis.

Obremenitvena ehokardiografija. Dvodimenzionalni ehokardiogram snemamo v različnih presekih pred obremenitvijo, med obremenitvijo in po njej. Obremenitev je bodisi fizična na tekočem traku ali sobnem kolesu bodisi farmakološka (npr. z dobutaminom).

Question:
30. Using Doppler echocardiography and the modified Bernoulli equation, we noninvasively estimate:
a. Any pressure gradient in the heart
b. Only the systolic pressure in the right ventricle
c. Only the gradient across a stenotic aortic valve
d. Central venous pressure
e. Only pulmonary arterial hypertension
Text Answer: Any pressure gradient in the heart
Reference: Interna medicina (6. izd., str. XXXVIII, 1693). (2022). Medicinska fakulteta; Buča.
Context:
Ocena tlakov. Doplerska ehokardiografija omogoča neinvazivne meritve gradientov tlakov skozi ustje zaklopk (slika 2.69). Pri toku krvi skozi ozko žilo se poveča pretočna hitrost, iz katere izmerimo razliko tlakov med sosednjima srčnima votlinama. Razmerje med razliko tlaka in hitrostjo izraža poenostavljena Bernoullijeva enačba: Δp = 4 × v²
Δp je razlika v tlaku preko zožitve (mmHg), v je največja hitrost pretoka za zožitev (m/s).
English: Pressure Assessment. Doppler echocardiography enables noninvasive measurement of pressure gradients across valve orifices (Fig. 2.69). As blood flows through a narrowed passage, the flow velocity increases; from this we calculate the pressure difference between the adjacent cardiac chambers. The relationship between pressure difference and velocity is expressed by the simplified Bernoulli equation:

Δp = 4 × v²

where Δp is the pressure difference across the stenosis (in mmHg) and v is the peak flow velocity through the narrowing (in m/s).

Question:
31. What is characteristic of cardiogenic shock?
a. Decreased central venous pressure
b. Decreased pulmonary arterial pressure
c. Decreased pulmonary capillary wedge pressure
d. Decreased mixed venous blood oxygen saturation
e. Decreased lactate concentration in arterial blood
Text Answer: Decreased mixed venous blood oxygen saturation
Reference: Interna medicina (6. izd., str. XXXVIII, 1693). (2022). Medicinska fakulteta; Buča.
Context: Diagnoza. Bolniki so hipotenzivni, hipoksemični, imajo presnovno acidozo s povišanim serumskim laktatom, moteno delovanje organov (motnje zavesti, oligurija). Diagnozo ob značilni klinični sliki lahko potrdimo z merjenjem hemodinamičnih spremenljivk, ki omogočajo tudi najbolj natančno specifično zdravljenje. Ugotovimo povišane polnitvene tlake ponavadi obeh prekatov, znižan minutni srčni volumen in povečano periferno ekstrakcijo kisika, ki se odraža z znižano zasičenostjo mešane venske krvi s kisikom. English: Diagnosis. Patients are hypotensive and hypoxemic, have metabolic acidosis with elevated serum lactates, and exhibit organ dysfunction (altered consciousness, oliguria). The diagnosis suggested by these clinical signs can be confirmed by measuring hemodynamic variables, which also guide the most precise, targeted treatment. Findings include elevated filling pressures upstream of both ventricles, reduced cardiac output, and increased peripheral oxygen extraction, reflected by decreased mixed venous blood oxygen saturation.

Question:
1. Which statement about the concentration test and desmopressin is correct?
a) The patient will be treated with dDAVP
b) The patient will be treated with lithium
c) The patient has nephrogenic diabetes insipidus
d) The patient has central diabetes insipidus
e) The patient has psychogenic polydipsia
Text Answer: The patient will be treated with dDAVP
Reference: Interna medicina (6. izd., str. XXXVIII, 1693). (2022). Medicinska fakulteta; Buča.
Context:
Central diabetes insipidus is treated with a synthetic AVP analogue, desmopressin. It acts predominantly on arginine-vasopressin V₂ receptors in the kidneys and only minimally on V₁ receptors in the vascular wall. In our practice, we use a lyophilized formulation, which the patient takes as a sublingual tablet every 12 hours; dosing every 8 hours is rarely necessary. In milder forms of the disease, a single evening dose of 1–2 tablets (60–120 µg) may suffice. Desmopressin is also available as conventional 200 µg tablets, which patients take and dose in a similar manner. The total daily requirement of desmopressin varies greatly: the range of lyophilizate doses is 60–720 µg per day, while conventional tablets range from 100 to 1000 µg per day. Desmopressin (dDAVP) is twice as potent as AVP and has a 12-hour duration of action, with minimal vasopressor effect.
Dosing must be individualized for each patient by gradually titrating and monitoring fluid balance, serum osmolality, and urine osmolality. Polyuria is reduced, and nocturia is eliminated; however, one does not attempt to normalize total diuresis (to less than 2 L/day), because there is a risk of hormone overdose and consequent water intoxication (hyponatremia). The medication has almost no side effects; only very rarely do patients experience a transient sensation of head pressure, paleness, and a rapid heartbeat (vasopressor effect) shortly after dosing.

Question:
3. Which of the following is true about glucocorticoid-induced osteoporosis?
a) It does not develop with glucocorticoid nasal spray use
b) Glucocorticoids accelerate resorption but do not reduce bone formation
c) We initiate treatment with bisphosphonates when bone mineral density is below –1 SD
d) Due to increased calcium excretion, only vitamin D is added, not calcium
e) Treatment begins only if glucocorticoids are expected to be given for more than 6 months
Text Answer: We initiate treatment with bisphosphonates when bone mineral density is below –1 SD
Reference: Harrison’s Principles of Internal Medicine, 20th ed.
Context:
“Osteoporosis is the most common long-term complication of systemic glucocorticoid therapy and is both preventable and partially reversible. Glucocorticoid-induced bone loss occurs rapidly within the first 6–12 months of therapy. Both increased bone resorption and decreased bone formation contribute to loss of bone mass.”
“Prophylaxis should include adequate intake of calcium (1,200–1,500 mg/day) and vitamin D (800–1,000 IU/day), as well as initiation of bisphosphonate therapy (e.g., alendronate 5–10 mg/day or risedronate 5 mg/day orally) in adults expected to receive the equivalent of ≥5 mg/day of prednisone for ≥3 months.”
“Inhaled glucocorticoids (e.g., nasal sprays) have minimal systemic absorption and generally do not cause glucocorticoid-induced osteoporosis.”

Question:
23. What is the approximate mortality rate in untreated hyperthyroid crisis?
a) Below 5%
b) Between 20% and 50%
c) 0% with modern treatment
d) Between 5% and 10%
e) Above 80%
Reference: Interna medicina (6. izd., str. XXXVIII, 1693). (2022). Medicinska fakulteta; Buča.
Context:
THYROID STORM (Hyperthyroid Crisis)
Epidemiology. It can occur in any form of hyperthyroidism, but fortunately it is very rare. Mortality ranges from 10 to 75 percent. It usually follows long-standing, untreated hyperthyroidism. Common precipitating factors include infection, unprepared surgical intervention, severe psychological stress, acute psychosis, trauma, childbirth, and iodinated contrast exposure. The pathophysiological mechanisms are not yet fully understood.

Question:
24. What applies to treatment with radioactive iodine (I-131)?
a) Only gamma rays are used
b) Beta rays cause cellular necrosis
c) Treatment is unsafe
d) It is contraindicated in all elderly patients
e) It is preferred in pregnant women
Reference: Interna medicina (6. izd., str. XXXVIII, 1693). (2022). Medicinska fakulteta; Buča.
Context: Treatment. The method of choice is administration of ^131I, which is a simple, effective, and safe treatment. After oral intake, it is rapidly and completely absorbed from the upper small intestine and accumulates in the autonomous thyroid tissue. During radioactive decay, more than 90% of ^131I emits beta particles that have a tissue penetration of 1 to 2 mm. These cause cell destruction in the autonomous tissue, while normal thyroid tissue receives only a minimal radiation dose. The ultimate effect of ^131I treatment is fibrosis of thyroid cells, and the volume of the nodule or thyroid is markedly reduced.

Question:
25. What is typical for autonomous thyroid tissue?
a) It is under normal regulatory control
b) It results from rapid thyroid cell turnover
c) It is more common in iodine-rich regions
d) It results from mutations in the TSH receptor or G-protein
e) It is suppressed by high iodine intake
Text Answer: It results from mutations in the TSH receptor or G-protein
Reference: Interna medicina (6. izd., str. XXXVIII, 1693). (2022). Medicinska fakulteta; Buča.
Context: Etiopathogenesis. A variety of different mutations in the genes for TSH receptor (TSH-R) or the G protein have been identified. The consequence of these mutations is activation of adenylate cyclase and continuous synthesis and secretion of thyroid hormones that are independent of TSH.

Question:
26. What should be the next step for a patient on long-term amiodarone who develops hypothyroidism?
a) Stop amiodarone immediately
b) Start methimazole
c) Start glucocorticoids
d) Begin treatment with L-thyroxine
e) Monitor without intervention
Reference: Harrison’s Principles of Internal Medicine, 20th ed.
Context: “Amiodarone‐induced hypothyroidism (AIH) occurs in susceptible individuals due to the iodine load and direct cytotoxic effects on thyroid follicular cells. In most patients who develop AIH, continuation of amiodarone is recommended if clinically necessary, because the cardiovascular benefits often outweigh the risk. The appropriate management is to initiate levothyroxine replacement to restore euthyroidism. Amiodarone need not be stopped unless there are severe extrathyroidal toxicities. The usual starting dose of levothyroxine is 50–100 μg daily, adjusted to maintain serum free T4 in the upper half of the reference range and normalize serum TSH.”

Question:
27. What is used to treat hyperthyroidism caused by iodine excess (Jod-Basedow phenomenon)?
a) L-thyroxine
b) Radioactive iodine
c) High-dose thionamides (antithyroid drugs)
d) Beta blockers alone
e) Prednisone
Reference: Interna medicina (6. izd., str. XXXVIII, 1693). (2022). Medicinska fakulteta; Buča.
Context: Treatment. In hyperthyroidism due to excess iodine, it is essential to discontinue intake of any iodine-containing substance. Hyperthyroidism is treated with antithyroid drugs (methimazole, propylthiouracil) in high doses and with agents that block iodine uptake in the thyroid. If amiodarone-induced type II hyperthyroidism is suspected, a glucocorticoid is added. Treatment is often challenging, and euthyroidism is usually achieved only after several months. When the clinical course is complicated and conservative measures fail to improve the patient, early thyroidectomy is indicated.

Question:
28. Which of the following is a known effect of amiodarone on the thyroid?
a) It has a short half-life
b) It is structurally unrelated to thyroid hormones
c) It contains a large amount of iodine
d) It depletes the body of iodine
e) It causes only hypothyroidism
Text Answer: It contains a large amount of iodine
Reference: Interna medicina (6. izd., str. XXXVIII, 1693). (2022). Medicinska fakulteta; Buča.
Context: Amiodarone is an effective antiarrhythmic that contains 37% iodine (75 mg per 200 mg tablet). It is lipophilic and accumulates in the body mainly in adipose tissue, myocardium, liver, cornea, and thyroid. From these stores, it is released slowly, and the iodide it releases remains a source of iodine load even after discontinuation of therapy. Its elimination half-life from the body is between 30 and 100 days. Because its structure is similar to thyroid hormones (see Figure 5.17), it competitively inhibits the type I deiodinase enzyme and thus the conversion of T₄ to T₃ in tissues. Consequently, during chronic treatment with amiodarone—even when serum free T₄ is in the upper normal range or slightly elevated—serum free T₃ is often in the lower normal range or decreased.

Question:
1. The source of massive bleeding from a duodenal ulcer is most often:
A. superior mesenteric artery
B. portal vein
C. inferior mesenteric artery
D. gastroduodenal artery
E. inferior vena cava
Reference: Harrison’s Principles of Internal Medicine, 20th ed.
Context: Due to its proximity to the posterior wall of the first part of the duodenum, the gastroduodenal artery is one of the most important sources of upper gastrointestinal bleeding—mostly secondary to peptic ulcer disease

Question:
2. Which mucosal change represents a precancerous lesion?
A. villous adenoma of the rectum
B. polyps in Peutz-Jeghers syndrome
C. angiodysplasia
D. juvenile polyps in the colon
E. solitary pedunculated polyp in the sigmoid colon, 5 mm in diameter
Reference: Interna medicina (6. izd., str. XXXVIII, 1693). (2022). Medicinska fakulteta; Buča.
Context: Klinična slika. Adenomi so večinoma asimptomatski. Približno 20 odstotkov jih prikrito krvavi in dajejo pozitiven hematest v blatu. Krvavitve navadno povzročajo večji polipi, ki imajo na površini ulceracijo. Redko povzročajo intususcepcijo. Viložni adenomi lahko povzročijo hipersekretorne sindrome, ki povzročajo hipokalemijo in povečano izločanje mukusa; v tovrstnih polipih pogosteje vznikne karcinom. English: Clinical picture. Adenomas are mostly asymptomatic. Approximately 20 percent bleed covertly and result in a positive stool hemoccult test. Bleeding is usually caused by larger polyps that have surface ulceration. They rarely cause intussusception. Villous adenomas can cause hypersecretory syndromes, leading to hypokalemia and increased mucus secretion; carcinoma more frequently arises in such polyps.

Question:
3. In acute cholecystitis we observe:
A. A painful resistance under the right costal margin
B. A mass in the epigastrium
C. Polyuria
D. Leukopenia
E. Left-sided pleural effusion
Text Answer: A painful resistance under the right costal margin
Reference: Interna medicina (6. izd., str. XXXVIII, 1693). (2022). Medicinska fakulteta; Buča.
Context:
Klinična slika. Pri 75 odstotkih bolnikov je vodilni simptom nenaden napad hudih bolečin pod desnim rebrnim lokom, ki se širi okoli pasu navzad navzgor proti desni lopatici. Bolečina se lahko okrepi med dihanjem. Traja 12 do 18 ur ter ciklično narašča in pojenjuje. Bolniki pogosto bruhajo in imajo visoko vročino. Ravno čas trajanja bolečine (dlje kot 6 ur) nam pomaga pri razločevanju med bolečino zaradi holecistitisa ali biliarno koliko.
English: Clinical picture. In 75% of patients, the leading symptom is a sudden onset of severe pain below the right costal margin, radiating around the waist and upward toward the right scapula. The pain can intensify with breathing. It lasts 12 to 18 hours and cycles through periods of worsening and relief. Patients often vomit and have a high fever. The duration of the pain (lasting longer than 6 hours) helps to distinguish pain due to cholecystitis from biliary colic.

Context: Neokluzivna mezenterična ishemija. Simptomi se navadno razvijejo v nekaj dneh. Začnejo se z nejasno, blago trebušno bolečino, ki narašča, pridružijo se ji bruhanje in hemohezije. Zgodnje simptome in znake lahko zastre osnovno obolenje, ki pogosto že samo terja intenzivno zdravljenje. English: Nonocclusive mesenteric ischemia. Symptoms typically develop over several days. They begin with vague, mild abdominal pain that gradually intensifies, accompanied by vomiting and hematochezia. Early symptoms and signs may be masked by the underlying disease, which itself often requires intensive treatment.

Question:
5. Hematemesis is:
A. vomiting of feculent material
B. passing of fresh blood
C. vomiting of bile
D. vomiting of blood
E. passing of black, tarry stools
Reference: Interna medicina (6. izd., str. XXXVIII, 1693). (2022). Medicinska fakulteta; Buča.
Context: Klinična slika. Za akutno krvavitev iz zgornje prebavne cevi sta značilni hematemeza (bruhanje sveže krvi ali kavne vsebine) in melena (črno mazavo blato z značilnim vonjem); za akutno krvavitev iz spodnje prebavne cevi je značilna hematohezija (sveža kri v blatu). English: Clinical picture. For acute bleeding from the upper gastrointestinal tract, hematemesis (vomiting of fresh blood or coffee-ground material) and melena (black tarry stool with a characteristic odor) are typical; for acute bleeding from the lower gastrointestinal tract, hematochezia (fresh blood in the stool) is typical.

Question:
8. What is Virchow’s node?
A. enlarged lymph node in the left supraclavicular fossa
B. enlarged lymph node in the left groin
C. enlarged lymph node in the right axilla
D. enlarged lymph node in the right groin
E. enlarged lymph node in the left axilla
Text Answer: enlarged lymph node in the left supraclavicular fossa
Reference: Interna medicina (6. izd., str. XXXVIII, 1693). (2022). Medicinska fakulteta; Buča.
Context:
Klinična slika. Zaradi neznačilnih težav bolezen običajno odkrijemo v napredovalem stadiju. Vodilna simptoma sta dispepsija in izguba telesne teže. Drugi simptomi, npr. vztrajajoče bruhanje, disfagija, sideropenična anemija in krvavitev, se navadno pojavijo ob lokalno napredovali bolezni.
Pri kliničnem pregledu običajno ne najdemo posebnosti. Pri napredovali bolezni zatipamo povečano bezgavko levo nad ključnico (Virchowova bezgavka) ali pod levo pazduho (Irishova bezgavka). Lahko zatipamo tumor v predelu popka (tumor sestre Marije Joseph), v epigastriju ali zgornjem delu trebuha ali tumor v mali medenici v predelu jajčnikov (Krukenbergov tumor). Zaradi peritonealnih zasevkov v mali medenici (Blumerjev znak) pride do delne ali popolne zapore v danki. Plevralni izliv ali ascites nastaneta zaradi karcinoze plevre ali peritoneja. Rak želodca je eden redkih malignih tumorjev, ki lahko povzroči karcinomski meningitis in številne paraneoplastične sindrome, kot so akantozis nigricans, dermatomiozitis, pemfigoid in cerebelarna ataksija. English: Clinical picture. Due to non-specific symptoms, the disease is usually discovered at an advanced stage. The leading symptoms are dyspepsia and weight loss. Other symptoms, such as persistent vomiting, dysphagia, sideropenic anemia, and bleeding, typically appear when the disease is locally advanced.
On clinical examination, no specific findings are usually detected. In advanced disease, an enlarged lymph node above the left clavicle (Virchow's node) or under the left armpit (Irish node) may be palpated. A tumor may also be palpated in the region of the umbilicus (Sister Mary Joseph nodule), in the epigastrium or upper abdomen, or in the pelvis at the level of the ovaries (Krukenberg tumor). Due to peritoneal metastases in the pelvis (Blumer’s sign), there may be partial or complete obstruction in the rectum. Pleural effusion or ascites can occur due to carcinomatosis of the pleura or peritoneum. Gastric cancer is one of the few malignant tumors that can cause carcinomatous meningitis and numerous paraneoplastic syndromes, such as acanthosis nigricans, dermatomyositis, pemphigoid, and cerebellar ataxia.

Lahko zatipamo tumor v predelu popka (tumor sestre Marije Joseph), v epigastriju ali zgornjem delu trebuha ali tumor v mali medenici v predelu jajčnikov (Krukenbergov tumor). Zaradi peritonealnih zasevkov v mali medenici (Blumerjev znak) pride do delne ali popolne zapore v danki. Plevralni izliv ali ascites nastaneta zaradi karcinoze plevre ali peritoneja. Rak želodca je eden redkih malignih tumorjev, ki lahko povzroči karcinomski meningitis in številne paraneoplastične sindrome, kot so akantozis nigricans, dermatomiozitis, pemfigoid in cerebelarna ataksija. English: Clinical picture. Due to non-specific symptoms, the disease is usually discovered at an advanced stage. The leading symptoms are dyspepsia and weight loss. Other symptoms, such as persistent vomiting, dysphagia, sideropenic anemia, and bleeding, typically appear when the disease is locally advanced.
On clinical examination, no specific findings are usually detected. In advanced disease, an enlarged lymph node above the left clavicle (Virchow's node) or under the left armpit (Irish node) may be palpated. A tumor may also be palpated in the region of the umbilicus (Sister Mary Joseph nodule), in the epigastrium or upper abdomen, or in the pelvis at the level of the ovaries (Krukenberg tumor). Due to peritoneal metastases in the pelvis (Blumer’s sign), there may be partial or complete obstruction in the rectum. Pleural effusion or ascites can occur due to carcinomatosis of the pleura or peritoneum. Question:
11. Mark the most common method of conservative treatment of chronic pancreatitis!
A. treatment of ascites
B. pain relief
C. treatment of steatorrhea
D. treatment of diabetes
E. pancreatic enzyme replacement
Reference: Interna medicina (6. izd., str. XXXVIII, 1693). (2022). Medicinska fakulteta; Buča.
Context: Zdravljenje bolečine pri kroničnem pankreatitisu je zelo zapleteno in individualno, vedno na nujno potrebno, saj bolečina pomembno znižuje kakovost življenja. Zdravljenje bolečine je najprej medikamentozno. Začnemo s paracetamolom in nesteroidnimi protivnetnimi učinkovinami, nadaljujemo s tramadolom. Velikokrat so potrebni močni opioidi, ki jih dajemo oralno. Če opioid nišo učinkoviti, jih ukine­mo. English: Pain management in chronic pancreatitis is very complex and must be individualized, always used as clinically necessary, since pain significantly reduces quality of life. Treatment of pain is primarily pharmacologic. We begin with paracetamol and nonsteroidal anti-inflammatory drugs, then proceed to tramadol. Often, potent opioids are required, which are given orally. If an opioid is not sufficiently effective, it is discontinued.

Question:
12. In Zollinger-Ellison syndrome, excessive secretion of:
a) gastrin
b) somatostatin
c) VIP
d) glucagon
e) cholecystokinin
Reference: Interna medicina (6. izd., str. XXXVIII, 1693). (2022). Medicinska fakulteta; Buča.
Context:
Gastrinomi, ki izločajo gastrin in povzročajo Zollinger–Ellisonov sindrom, so običajno majhni, multipli tumorji v dvanajstniku. Pogosto metastazirajo v lokalne bezgavke že pred odkritjem primarnega tumorja, kar ne pomeni nujno slabe prognoze bolezni. English: 
Gastrinomas, which secrete gastrin and cause Zollinger–Ellison syndrome, are usually small, multiple tumors in the duodenum. They often metastasize to local lymph nodes even before the primary tumor is detected, which does not necessarily imply a poor prognosis

Question:
13. Pseudomembranous colitis is associated with the causative factor:
a) use of antibiotics
b) use of diuretics
c) use of beta-blockers
d) use of laxatives
e) intravenous drug injection
Reference: Harrison’s Principles of Internal Medicine, 20th ed.
Context: Clostridioides difficile is the most common cause of antibiotic-associated diarrhea and is responsible for virtually all cases of pseudomembranous colitis. The use of antibiotics, particularly clindamycin, cephalosporins, and fluoroquinolones, disrupts the normal gut flora, allowing C. difficile to proliferate and produce toxins that lead to colitis.

Question:
14. The extraintestinal manifestations of Crohn's disease DO NOT include:
a) arthritis
b) ankylosing spondylitis
c) iritis
d) atopic dermatitis
e) erythema nodosum
Reference: Interna medicina (6. izd., str. XXXVIII, 1693). (2022). Medicinska fakulteta; Buča.
Context: Klinična slika in postavitev diagnoze. Na kronično vnetno črevesno bolezen posumimo pri bolniku s kronično drisko, ki je lahko krvava, s pridruženimi bolečinami v trebuhu, anemijo, malabsorpcijo. Pri vsaj tretjini bolnikov so prisotne tudi zunajčrevesne manifestacije bolezni, tj. prizadetost sklepov, kože in oči. Za postavitev diagnoze je potreben endoskopski pregled prebavil, pri katerem odvzamemo biopsije za patohistološko preiskavo, dodatno si pomagamo z laboratorijskimi in slikovnimi preiskavami (magnetresonančna enterografija, računalniška tomografija, ultrazvočni pregled črevesja). Diferencialno diagnozo prikazuje tabela 4.24. English: Clinical picture and establishing the diagnosis. Chronic inflammatory bowel disease is suspected in patients with chronic diarrhea, which may be bloody, accompanied by abdominal pain, anemia, and malabsorption. In at least one third of patients, extraintestinal manifestations of the disease are present, i.e., involvement of the joints, skin, and eyes. Establishing the diagnosis requires an endoscopic examination of the digestive tract, during which biopsies are taken for histopathological examination. Additional assistance comes from laboratory and imaging studies (magnetic resonance enterography, computed tomography, ultrasound of the bowel). The differential diagnosis is shown in Table 4.24.

Question:
15. The most common cause of acute pancreatitis:
a. choledocholithiasis (bile duct stones)
b. infection
c. hereditary pancreatitis
d. hypercalcemia
e. hyperlipidemia
Reference: Interna medicina (6. izd., str. XXXVIII, 1693). (2022). Medicinska fakulteta; Buča.
Context: Etiologija. Najpogostejši vzrok za nastanek pankreatitisa so žolčni kamni in prekomerno pitje alkohola (več kot 80 odstotkov primerov), drugi vzroki so redki (tabela 4.45). Nastane pri 3 do 7 odstotkih bolnikov z žolčnimi kamni in pri 10 odstotkih ljudi, ki prekomerno uživajo alkohol. English: Etiology. The most common causes of pancreatitis are gallstones and excessive alcohol consumption (more than 80 percent of cases), with other causes being rare (table 4.45). It occurs in 3 to 7 percent of patients with gallstones and in 10 percent of people who excessively consume alcohol.

Question:
16. The most common causes of gastric and duodenal peptic ulcers are:
a. treatment with nonsteroidal anti-inflammatory drugs (NSAIDs)
b. Zollinger-Ellison syndrome

d. treatment with calcium channel blockers
e. treatment with vitamin K antagonists
Reference: Interna medicina (6. izd., str. XXXVIII, 1693). (2022). Medicinska fakulteta; Buča.
Context:
Poglavitni povzročitelji peptičnih razjed so H. pylori in nesteroidna protivnetna zdravila (tabela 4.10). Peptične razjede se pojavljajo takrat, kadar agresivno delovanje solne kisline in pepsina prevlada obrambe mehanizme sluznice zgornjih prebavil. Etiologija nastanka peptične razjede je mnogokrat multifaktorska, pomembno vlogo imajo tudi dejavniki okolja. English: 
The principal causes of peptic ulcers are Helicobacter pylori and nonsteroidal anti-inflammatory drugs (see Table 4.10). Peptic ulcers develop when the aggressive action of gastric acid and pepsin overcomes the defensive mechanisms of the upper gastrointestinal mucosa. The etiology of peptic ulcer formation is often multifactorial, with environmental factors also playing an important role.

Question:
What is the limit for lower-risk drinking for men in Slovenia?

a) Up to 7 units of alcohol weekly or up to 3 units on a single drinking occasion.
b) Up to 10 units of alcohol weekly or up to 4 units on a single drinking occasion.
c) Up to 14 units of alcohol weekly or up to 5 units on a single drinking occasion.
d) Up to 21 units of alcohol weekly, with no limit per occasion.
e) No specific limits, as long as it doesn't lead to intoxication.
Text Answer: Up to 14 units of alcohol weekly or up to 5 units on a single drinking occasion.

Question:
For how many days retrospectively can a personal doctor issue sick leave in Slovenia?

a) 30 days
b) 14 days
c) 7 days
d) 3 days
e) No retrospective issuance is allowed.
Reference: Družinska medicina, Združenja zdravnikov družinske medicine - SZD 2012
Context:
"Glede na dolžino bolniške odsotnosti so tudi drugačni postopki urejanja zadeve. Od 1. marca 2022 dolžino bolniške odsotnosti lahko delimo na:
Kratkotrajna bolniška odsotnost – do 3 dni brez potrdila zdravnika
Bolniška odsotnost – do 20 dni. Bolniški stalež vam odobri osebni zdravnik, prav tako pa vam izda tudi bolniški list. Prvih 20 dni odsotnosti je zaposleni upravičen do nadomestila plače, ki je v celoti breme delodajalca.
Daljša bolniška odsotnost – nad 20 dni. Daljši bolniški stalež presoja komisija, ki jo imenuje ZZZS. Ta zaposlenemu izda odločbo, v kateri so navedeni podatki o razlogu odsotnosti, datum izteka nadomestila in režim gibanja zaposlenega v času odsotnosti. Plačilo bolniške odsotnosti do 20 dni gre v breme delodajalca, nad 20 dni pa v breme ZZZS." English: "Depending on the length of the sick leave, there are also different procedures for handling the matter. From March 1, 2022, the length of sick leave can be divided into:
Short-term sick leave - up to 3 days without a doctor's certificate
Sick leave - up to 20 days. Your personal doctor approves your sick leave, and you are also issued a sick leave certificate. For the first 20 days of absence, the employee is entitled to salary compensation, which is entirely the employer's responsibility.
Longer sick leave - over 20 days. Longer sick leave is assessed by a commission appointed by the ZZZS. This commission issues the employee a decision stating the reason for the absence, the date the compensation expires, and the employee's movement regime during the absence. Payment for sick leave up to 20 days is the responsibility of the employer, and over 20 days is the responsibility of the ZZZS."

Question:
What is the most important predictive factor for the development of diabetic foot?

a) Uncontrolled glucose concentration
b) Tight shoes
c) Foot injury
d) Elevated cholesterol
e) Duration of diabetes mellitus
Reference: Interna medicina (6. izd., str. XXXVIII, 1693). (2022). Medicinska fakulteta; Buča.
Context:
Paliativna oskrba (palliare, lat. ogrniti). Po definiciji Svetov ne zdravstvene organizacije (iz leta 2002) paliativna oskrba 
preprečuje in lajša telesno, psihično, socialno in duhovno 
trpljenje bolnikov z napredovalo življenje ogrožajočo kro nično boleznijo in njihovih bližnjih. Cilj paliativne oskrbe je 
izboljšanje kakovosti življenja bolnikov z napredovalo kro nično boleznijo in njihovih bližnjih. Po novi, na konsenzu 
temelječi definiciji iz leta 2020 smo dolžni paliativno oskrbo 
nuditi tudi ostalim bolnikom, ki se soočajo s kroničnim tr pljenjem, čeprav se jim življenje še ne izteka (zgodnja palia tivna oskrba) oziroma bodo morda celo ozdraveli. English: Palliative care (palliare, Latin: to cover). According to the definition of the World Health Organization (2002), palliative care 
prevents and alleviates physical, psychological, social and spiritual 
suffering of patients with advanced life-threatening chronic illness and their loved ones. The goal of palliative care is 
to improve the quality of life of patients with advanced chronic illness and their loved ones. According to the new, consensus-based definition from 2020, we are obliged to offer palliative care 
also to other patients who are facing chronic suffering, even though their life is not yet ending (early palliative care) or may even be cured.

Question:
1. Which histological type of bladder cancer is the most common?
a. Urothelial carcinoma
b. Squamous cell carcinoma
c. Adenocarcinoma
d. Mesothelioma
e. Small cell carcinoma
Reference: Strojan, P., & Hočevar, M. (Eds.). (2018). Onkologija: Učbenik za študente medicine [Oncology: Textbook for medical students] (1st ed.). Onkološki inštitut Ljubljana.
Context: The most common histological type is urothelial (transitional cell) carcinoma (over 90%), with squamous cell carcinoma (6–8%) and adenocarcinoma (2%) being less frequent. Urothelial carcinomas are classified by degree of differentiation according to two systems, both of which are in use. According to the WHO 1973 classification, they are divided into: G1 – well differentiated, G2 – moderately differentiated, G3 – poorly differentiated. According to the WHO 1998 classification, they are divided into: papillary urothelial neoplasm of low malignant potential, low-grade urothelial carcinoma, high-grade urothelial carcinoma.

Question:
2. Which screening test is most widely used worldwide for early detection of cervical cancer?
a. Visual inspection with acetic acid (VIA)
b. HPV DNA testing
c. Endocervical curettage
d. Papanicolaou (Pap) smear
e. Colposcopic biopsy
Reference: Strojan, P., & Hočevar, M. (Eds.). (2018). Onkologija: Učbenik za študente medicine [Oncology: Textbook for medical students] (1st ed.). Onkološki inštitut Ljubljana.
Context: Program ZORA (http://zora.onko-i.si/): Cervical cancer (CC) is one of the few cancers that can be prevented by detecting and treating precancerous lesions. The examination of cervical cells in a smear was first described in 1941 by Papanicolaou and Traut, and is therefore also called the Pap test. The test began to be adopted in the 1950s as a screening tool for detecting precancerous changes in apparently healthy women.

Question:
3. What is the most common malignancy in adult women in Slovenia?
a. Ovarian cancer
b. Cervical cancer
c. Lung cancer
d. Breast cancer
e. Endometrial cancer
Reference: Strojan, P., & Hočevar, M. (Eds.). (2018). Onkologija: Učbenik za študente medicine [Oncology: Textbook for medical students] (1st ed.). Onkološki inštitut Ljubljana.
Context: Breast cancer is the most common cancer among women in the developed world, accounting for the leading cancer in 140 out of 184 countries, making it an important public health issue. The estimate for 2012 was 1.7 million new cases worldwide. The risk of breast cancer generally depends on the country’s level of development, with rates decreasing from north to south and from west to east in Europe. In 2012, the incidence rate of breast cancer in Belgium was 148/100,000, in Denmark 143/100,000, and in Slovenia, for the period 2008–2012, it was 117/100,000 inhabitants. In Slovenia, the incidence also decreases from west to east across the country. For 2015, it was estimated that 1,287 new cases of breast cancer would be detected.

Question:
4. After leukemia, which two solid tumors are most common in children?
a. Hodgkin lymphoma and non-Hodgkin lymphoma
b. Neuroblastoma and Wilms tumor
c. Brain tumors (especially medulloblastoma) and neuroblastoma
d. Osteosarcoma and Ewing sarcoma
e. Rhabdomyosarcoma and hepatoblastoma
Reference: Strojan, P., & Hočevar, M. (Eds.). (2018). Onkologija: Učbenik za študente medicine [Oncology: Textbook for medical students] (1st ed.). Onkološki inštitut Ljubljana.
Context: The most common type of cancer in children is leukemia (30%); among solid tumors, central nervous system tumors are the most common (21%), followed by lymphomas (12%). Other solid tumors are rarer, such as neuroblastoma (7%), soft tissue sarcoma (7%), bone sarcoma (7%), nephroblastoma (7%), retinoblastoma (3%), and germ cell tumors (3%).

Question:
5. Which tumor marker is most frequently elevated in hepatocellular carcinoma?
a. Carcinoembryonic antigen (CEA)
b. CA 19-9
c. Alpha-fetoprotein (AFP)
d. CA 125
e. Beta-2 microglobulin
Reference: Strojan, P., & Hočevar, M. (Eds.). (2018). Onkologija: Učbenik za študente medicine [Oncology: Textbook for medical students] (1st ed.). Onkološki inštitut Ljubljana.
Context: AFP (alpha-fetoprotein) is an oncofetal antigen that is present in the blood after birth at very low concentrations. It is characteristically elevated in patients with germ cell tumors and hepatocellular carcinoma. Elevated AFP levels in the serum can also be seen in adults with certain other malignant diseases (pancreatic cancer, lung cancer, gastric cancer) and non-malignant diseases (acute viral hepatitis, liver cirrhosis, obstructive jaundice).

Question:
6. In women under age 35, which imaging modality is first-line to distinguish cystic from solid breast lesions?
a. Mammography
b. MRI of the breast
c. Breast ultrasound
d. PET-CT
e. Contrast-enhanced CT of the chest
Reference: Strojan, P., & Hočevar, M. (Eds.). (2018). Onkologija: Učbenik za študente medicine [Oncology: Textbook for medical students] (1st ed.). Onkološki inštitut Ljubljana.
Context: Breast ultrasound is an excellent complementary diagnostic examination for assessing mammographic findings in the breasts, but it cannot replace mammography. It is used as the primary diagnostic tool only in young women up to the age of 35, in whom mammography is poorly informative due to the dense breast tissue.

Question:
7. What is the approximate five-year overall survival rate for patients with papillary thyroid carcinoma in Slovenia?
a. 50 %
b. 60 %
c. 70 %
d. 80 %
e. ≥ 90 %
Reference: Strojan, P., & Hočevar, M. (Eds.). (2018). Onkologija: Učbenik za študente medicine [Oncology: Textbook for medical students] (1st ed.). Onkološki inštitut Ljubljana.
Context: The prognosis for patients with papillary and follicular thyroid cancer in Slovenia is very good, as the relative five-year survival rate for women exceeds 95%. The survival rate for men is only slightly lower, at 91%. Even after the first five years, the course of the disease remains favorable in most patients, with a reported thirty-year survival rate of 95% as well.

a. MEN 1
b. MEN 2A
c. MEN 2B
d. Li–Fraumeni syndrome
e. Von Hippel–Lindau syndrome
Reference: Strojan, P., & Hočevar, M. (Eds.). (2018). Onkologija: Učbenik za študente medicine [Oncology: Textbook for medical students] (1st ed.). Onkološki inštitut Ljubljana.
