It is important for excluding other causes of the patient’s symptoms and signs. In patients with pulmonary infarction, we typically see opacities of triangular shape (the apex of the triangular opacity points toward the lung hilum). The costophrenic angle on the affected side is often blunted due to a pleural effusion. Le redko opazimo odsotnost pljučnega žilja v prizadetem polju pljuč (oligemija, Westermarkov znak) - referring to pulmonary embolism

Question:
11. For typical pain in aortic dissection, the characteristic features are:
a. It is severe, tearing
b. It is mild, stabbing
c. Pain begins suddenly
d. Pain develops slowly
e. Pain can migrate along the course of the aorta
f. It changes with breathing and body position
g. Pain most often radiates to the jaw
h. Pain most often radiates to the back

1. a + c + e + h 
2. a + c + f + g
3. b + d + g + h
4. a + d + e + h
5. b + c + e + f
Text Answer: 1. For typical pain in aortic dissection, the characteristic features are:
Reference: Interna medicina (6. izd., str. XXXVIII, 1693). (2022). Medicinska fakulteta; Buča.
Context: Klinična slika. Pri akutni disekciji aorte je v ospredju hitro nastala, huda, ostra ali trgajoča bolečina v prsnem košu, ki seva pogosto v hrbet. Bolečina je stalna, lahko se s časom seli po arterah navzdol. Pri kronični disekciji ni bolečine. Kadar zapre disekcija vejo aorte, lahko povzroči srčni infarkt, obsežno možgansko kap, ishemijo katere koli okončine, ledvic, ishemijo mezenterikalnega področja z gangreno črevesja ali pa prekine vertebralne arterije s paraplegijo. Ob bolečini ima večina bolnikov povišan krvni tlak, če pa izmerimo nizek tlak, gre lahko za meritev na okončini z oslabljeno prekrvavitvijo zaradi disekcije (psevdohipotenzija), rupturo aorte, tamponado srca ali pa akutno insuficienco aortne zaklopke. Pri tamponadi srca so prekomerno napete vratne vene, pri akutni aortni insuficienci slišimo protomezodiastolični šum nad aortno zaklopko. Ob disekciji lahko nastane plevralni izlivi, navadno levorostranski, ki je lahko posledica vnetne reakcije ali pa krvavitve v plevralni prostor. Redko pride zaradi pritiska na nervus recurrens do hripavosti in Hornerjevega sindroma. English: Clinical presentation. In acute aortic dissection, the hallmark is a sudden onset of severe, sharp or tearing chest pain that often radiates to the back. The pain is constant and may migrate downward along the arterial tree over time. In chronic dissection, pain is absent. If the dissection occludes an aortic branch, it can result in myocardial infarction, a major stroke, ischemia of any extremity, renal ischemia, mesenteric ischemia with bowel gangrene, or vertebral artery obstruction leading to paraplegia. Most patients are hypertensive at the time of pain, but if hypotension is measured, it may be due to measuring on a limb with compromised perfusion from the dissection (pseudohypotension), aortic rupture, cardiac tamponade, or acute aortic valve insufficiency. In tamponade, the jugular veins are markedly distended; in acute aortic insufficiency, a protodiastolic murmur is heard over the aortic valve. Dissection may also lead to pleural effusions—typically left‐sided—owing to inflammation or bleeding into the pleural space. Rarely, compression of the recurrent laryngeal nerve causes hoarseness or a Horner’s syndrome.

Question:
12. Which of the following is a contraindication to treating chronic heart failure with β-blockers?
a. Resting heart rate < 70 /min
b. Hypokalemia (< 3.5 mmol/L)
c. Second-degree atrioventricular block
d. Ankle edema
e. Left ventricular ejection fraction < 30 %
Reference: Interna medicina (6. izd., str. XXXVIII, 1693). (2022). Medicinska fakulteta; Buča.
Context: Kontraindikacije za zdravljenje z blokatorji receptorjev beta so neurjena astma, atrioventrikularni blok druge stopnje Mobitz 2 ali tretje stopnje, bolezen sinusnega vozla in hipotenzija. Pri uvajanju in povečanju odmerkov blokatorjev receptorjev beta lahko redko pride do hipotenzije, bradikardije, atrioventrikularnih blokov ali poslabšanja srčnega popuščanja. English: Contraindications to β-blocker therapy include uncontrolled asthma, second-degree (Mobitz II) or third-degree atrioventricular block, sinus node disease, and hypotension. When initiating or up-titrating β-blockers, rare adverse effects can include hypotension, bradycardia, atrioventricular block, or worsening heart failure.

Question:
13. Which of the following is an electrocardiographic criterion for left ventricular hypertrophy?
a. Positive R wave in V₁
b. QRS complex duration > 125 ms
c. Right axis deviation
d. Small R wave in aVL
e. (S in V₁) + (R in V₅) > 35 mm (Sokolow–Lyon index)
Text Answer: (S in V₁) + (R in V₅) > 35 mm (Sokolow–Lyon index)
Reference: Interna medicina (6. izd., str. XXXVIII, 1693). (2022). Medicinska fakulteta; Buča.
Context: Merila za elektrokardiografsko diagnozo hipertrofije levega prekata so različna, najbolj znan je Sokolow–Lyonov indeks, ki govori za hipertrofijo, če je (S v V1) + (R v V5 ali V6) > 35 mm. Senzitivnost tega indeksa je okoli 40 odstotkov, specifičnost pa nad 90 odstotkov. English: The criteria for the electrocardiographic diagnosis of left ventricular hypertrophy are varied; the best‐known is the Sokolow–Lyon index, which indicates hypertrophy if the sum of the S‐wave in V₁ and the R‐wave in V₅ (or V₆) exceeds 35 mm. The sensitivity of this index is about 40%, while its specificity is over 90%.

Question:
14. Indications for transesophageal echocardiography include:
a. Suspicion of endocarditis
b. Annual follow-up in a patient with aortic stenosis
c. Assessment of regurgitation of a prosthetic mitral valve
d. Suspicion of an ascending aortic aneurysm
e. Evaluation of cardiac function in secondary systemic arterial hypertension

1. b + d + e
2. a + b + c
3. a + c + e
4. a + c + d 
5. c + d + e
Reference: Interna medicina (6. izd., str. XXXVIII, 1693). (2022). Medicinska fakulteta; Buča.
Context:
Tabela 2.4 Indikacije za transezofagealno ehokardiografijo
Iskanje kardiovaskularnih vzrokov embolizmov
Infekcijski endokarditis
Mitralna insuficienca
Pregled umetnih zaklopk
Nekatere prirojene bolezni srca
Vodenje perkutanih posegov na zaklopkah
Aortne anevrizme, disekcija aorte
Intraoperativni ultrazvok srca
Kritično bolni 
Slaba vidljivost pri transtorakalni ehokardiografski preiskavi

English: Table 2.4 Indications for Transesophageal Echocardiography
Investigation of cardiovascular sources of embolism
Infective endocarditis
Mitral regurgitation
Evaluation of prosthetic heart valves
Certain congenital heart diseases
Guidance of percutaneous valve procedures
Aortic aneurysms and aortic dissection
Intraoperative cardiac imaging
Critically ill patients
Poor acoustic windows on transthoracic echocardiography

Question:
15. Arrhythmogenic right ventricular cardiomyopathy (ARVC) is:
A. A result of replacement of the right ventricular myocardium with fat and connective tissue
B. A consequence of excessive alcohol consumption
C. Occurs only in patients with excessive body weight
D. A result of primary pulmonary hypertension
E. Associated with prolonged QT interval
Text Answer: A result of replacement of the right ventricular myocardium with fat and connective tissue
Reference: Interna medicina (6. izd., str. XXXVIII, 1693). (2022). Medicinska fakulteta; Buča.
Context:
ARITMOGENA KARDIOMIOPATIJA DESNEGA PREKATA
Za to bolezen je značilno napredujoče nadomeščanje miokarda desnega prekata z maščobnim in vezivnim tkivom. Sprva so spremembe ozko omejene, z napredovanjem bolezni pa zajamejo velik del desnega prekata in se lahko širijo tudi na levo stran srca. Za to bolezen so opisani različni načini dedovanja. Bolniki imajo pogoste aritmije. English: ARRHYTHMOGENIC RIGHT VENTRICULAR CARDIOMYOPATHY
This disease is characterized by the progressive replacement of the right ventricular myocardium with fatty and connective tissue. Initially, the changes are narrowly localized, but as the disease progresses, they involve a large part of the right ventricle and can also extend to the left side of the heart. Various inheritance patterns have been described for this disease. Patients frequently have arrhythmias.

Question:
16. For a stress test (exercise ECG) to be considered positive for ischemia, which of the following must occur?
a. Horizontal or down-sloping ST-segment depression on the ECG during exercise with concomitant chest pain
b. Up-sloping ST-segment depression on the ECG during exercise without chest pain
c. Systolic blood pressure rising to 200 mm Hg during exercise
d. The QT interval shortening by 20% during exercise
e. A bundle-branch block appearing during exercise
Text Answer: Horizontal or down-sloping ST-segment depression on the ECG during exercise with concomitant chest pain
Reference: Interna medicina (6. izd., str. XXXVIII, 1693). (2022). Medicinska fakulteta; Buča.
Context: Pri posameznikih s koronarno boleznijo se ob večji porabi kisika v srčni mišici med obremenitvijo lahko pojavijo značilne elektrokardiografske spremembe (slika 2.72). Najpogosteje zaznavamo znake subendokardne ishemije, ki se kaže z znižanjem (denivelacijo) veznice ST. Vodoravno oziroma padajoče znižanje veznice ST (horizontalno oziroma descendentno denivelacijo) za > 0,1 mV opredelimo kot ishemični odziv. English: In patients with coronary artery disease, increased myocardial oxygen demand during stress can produce characteristic electrocardiographic changes (Figure 2.72). Most commonly, we observe signs of subendocardial ischemia, manifested as ST-segment depression (denivelation). A horizontal or down-sloping ST-segment depression (horizontal or descending denivelation) of more than 0.1 mV is defined as an ischemic response.

Question:
17. Determine which heart chamber has the correctly stated normal pressure value.
a. The mean pressure in the right atrium is 1–5 mmHg
b. The systolic pressure in the pulmonary artery is 35–45 mmHg
c. The pulmonary capillary wedge (occlusion) pressure is 20–25 mmHg
d. The left ventricular end-diastolic pressure is 15–20 mmHg
e. The left ventricular end-diastolic pressure is 50–80 mmHg
Text Answer: The mean pressure in the right atrium is 1–5 mmHg
Reference: Interna medicina (6. izd., str. XXXVIII, 1693). (2022). Medicinska fakulteta; Buča.
Context:  XN+LN3Xk4ty8bP9+zDOf3pOGDg9l4/8l8/P8BwuHKvpra1m4AAAAASUVORK5CYII=

Question:
18. Which of the following is characteristic of cardiogenic shock?
a. It occurs in 2–3% of patients with acute myocardial infarction
b. Hospital mortality of patients is 20
c. Oxygen extraction in the tissues is decreased
d. Patients usually require endotracheal intubation and mechanical ventilation
e. Metabolic acidosis with a normal anion gap
Text Answer: Patients usually require endotracheal intubation and mechanical ventilation
Reference: Interna medicina (6. izd., str. XXXVIII, 1693). (2022). Medicinska fakulteta; Buča.
Context: Tipičen primer bolnika, ki ga je treba takoj intubirati in umetno ventilirati, je bolnik s srčnim infarktom, pljučnim edemom in kardiogenim šokom. English: A typical example of a patient who requires immediate intubation and mechanical ventilation is one with myocardial infarction, pulmonary edema, and cardiogenic shock.

Question:
19.For Wolff–Parkinson–White preexcitation, which of the following are true? (Choose the correct combination.)
a. Because conduction occurs via an accessory pathway, the PR interval is prolonged
b. In orthodromic tachycardia, the QRS complexes are normally narrow
c. In antidromic tachycardia, the QRS complexes are widened
d. Atrial fibrillation in WPW can be treated with digoxin
e. Atrial fibrillation in WPW can be treated with verapamil

1. a + e
2. d + e
3. b + c 
4. a + b
5. c + e
Reference: Interna medicina (6. izd., str. XXXVIII, 1693). (2022). Medicinska fakulteta; Buča.
Context:
V atrioventrikularni smeri poteka kroženje skoraj vedno po normalni, v ventrikulo-atrijski pa po akcesorni poti (ortodromna tahikardija); kompleksi QRS so ozki (razen pri anatomskem ali funkcionalnem kračnem bloku). Izjemoma poteka kroženje v obratni smeri (antidromna tahikardija); kompleksi QRS so široki.

English: In the atrioventricular direction, the reentrant circuit almost always conducts down the normal pathway and back up the accessory pathway (orthodromic tachycardia); the QRS complexes are narrow (unless there is an anatomical or functional hemiblock). Exceptionally, the circuit conducts in the reverse direction (antidromic tachycardia); the QRS complexes are wide.

Question:
20. How is induced hypothermia therapy performed in patients after cardiac arrest?
a. Apply cold packs to the patient’s head
b. Cool the patient’s core temperature to 35–36 °C for 6 hours
c. Cool the patient’s core temperature to 32–34 °C for 6 hours
d. Cool the patient’s core temperature to 32–34 °C for 24 hours
e. Cool the patient’s core temperature to 28 °C for 12 hours
Text Answer: Cool the patient’s core temperature to 32–34 °C for 24 hours
Reference: Interna medicina (6. izd., str. XXXVIII, 1693). (2022). Medicinska fakulteta; Buča.
Context:
Bolnika z infuzijami na štiri stopinje ohlajene fiziološke raztopine (30 ml/kg telesne mase v 30 minutah) ter oblaganjem z ledom v predelu vratu, pazduh in dimelj shladimo na ciljno temperaturo, ki je 32–34 °C. Bolnika moramo pri ohlajanju močno sedirati, po potrebi pa tudi relaksirati, da se ne bi z drgetanjem segreval. Bolnikovo temperaturo 24 ur vzdržujemo med 32 in 34 °C, nato pustimo, da se bolnik pasivno ogreje na normalno telesno temperaturo.

English Translation

The patient is cooled with infusions of normal saline chilled to 4 °C (30 mL/kg body weight over 30 minutes) and by applying ice packs to the neck, axillae, and groin until a target core temperature of 32–34 °C is reached. The patient must be deeply sedated—and if necessary paralyzed—to prevent shivering, which would generate heat. The core temperature is maintained at 32–34 °C for 24 hours, after which the patient is allowed to rewarm passively to normothermia. English: Patients with infusions at four degrees of cooled saline (30 ml/kg body weight in 30 minutes) and ice cladding in the neck, armpits and ingredients are cooled to the target temperature of 32-34 ° C.The patient should be seriously seduced when cooled and, if necessary, relaxed so as not to heat up with shivering.The patient's temperature is maintained between 32 and 34 ° C for 24 hours, then allowed to pass the patient passively to normal body temperature.

English Translation

The Patient is Cooled with Infusions of Normal Saline Chilled to 4 ° C (30 ml/kg body weight over 30 minutes) and by Applying Ice Packs to the Neck, Axille, and Grain Until A Target Core Temperature of 32-34 ° C is reached.The Patient must be deeply sedated -and if neecessary paralyzed - to Prevent Shivering, which would generates heat.The Core temperature is mainthained at 32-34 ° C for 24 Hours, after which the Patient is allowed to reWarm passively to normothermia.

Question:
21. Thrombolytic therapy is absolutely contraindicated in a patient:
A. with a peptic ulcer
B. with a severe head injury 5 days ago and an inserted ICP electrode
C. after cardiac arrest
D. who is being treated with aspirin
E. pregnant in the 6th month of pregnancy
Text Answer: with a severe head injury 5 days ago and an inserted ICP electrode
Reference: Harrison’s Principles of Internal Medicine (2022)
Context:
Table 273-5: Absolute Contraindications to Thrombolytic Therapy)
Recent head trauma (including severe injury with ICP monitoring)
Active bleeding
Suspected aortic dissection
Intracranial tumors or vascular lesions
Recent CNS surgery

Question:
22. Which auscultation finding in a patient with mitral stenosis is almost always present?
A. Low-frequency mid-diastolic rumble
B. Accentuated first heart sound
C. Presystolic murmur
D. High-frequency Graham Steell murmur
E. Holosystolic murmur at the apex
Reference: Interna medicina (6. izd., str. XXXVIII, 1693). (2022). Medicinska fakulteta; Buča.
Context: Pulmonalna sestavina drugega tona je poudarjena, če je že navzoča pljučna hipertenzija. Drugemu tonu po krajšem ali daljšem presledku sledi odpiralni ton mitralne zaklopke. Nato se sliši intervalni diastolični nizkofrekvenčni šum (mezodiastolično bobnenje), ki se presistolično (med sistolo preddvorov) okrepi. English: The pulmonary component of the second heart sound is accentuated if pulmonary hypertension is already present. After a shorter or longer interval following the second heart sound, the opening snap of the mitral valve is heard. Then an interval diastolic low-frequency murmur (mid-diastolic rumble) is heard, which becomes accentuated in the presystolic phase (during atrial systole).

Question:
23. In which patients do we need to be very careful when using spironolactone?
A. In hypertensive patients
B. In patients with renal failure
C. In patients who are also receiving ACE inhibitors and other diuretics
D. In diabetic patients with normal heart function
E. In patients with hypokalemia
1. A and B
2. B and C
3. C and D
4. B, C, and D
5. D and E
Text Answer: 3. In which patients do we need to be very careful when using spironolactone?
Reference: Harrison’s Principles of Internal Medicine (2022)
Page: Chapter 253: Heart Failure with Reduced Ejection Fraction (HFrEF)
Context: Mineralocorticoid receptor antagonists (MRAs), such as spironolactone, are recommended for patients with HFrEF who remain symptomatic despite optimal medical therapy. However, caution is advised in patients with impaired renal function or those receiving concomitant therapy with ACE inhibitors or ARBs due to the increased risk of hyperkalemia. Regular monitoring of renal function and serum potassium levels is essential in these patients.

Question:
24. The main mechanism of treating heart failure with cardiac resynchronization therapy (CRT) is:
A. Electroconversion
B. Mechanical coordination of left ventricular contraction
C. Shortening of the QRS duration and consequent mechanical synchronization of the left ventricle
D. Prevention of arrhythmias by antitachycardia pacing
E. Electrical stimulation to prevent bradycardias and the resulting cardiomyopathy
Text Answer: Shortening of the QRS duration and consequent mechanical synchronization of the left ventricle
Reference: Interna medicina (6. izd., str. XXXVIII, 1693). (2022). Medicinska fakulteta; Buča.
Context:
Z dvoprekatnim spodbujanjem naj bi zdravili bolnike s srčnim popuščanjem v funkcijskih razredih med NYHA II in IV, z iztisnim deležem levega prekata pod 35 odstotki in širino kompleksa QRS nad 130 ms. V raziskavah so imeli boljši odziv na tovrstno zdravljenje bolniki z levokračničnim blokom kot bolniki z desnokračničnim blokom. Zdravljenje srčnega popuščanja z dvoprekatnim spodbujanjem temelji na spoznanju, da se pri skoraj 30 odstotkih bolnikov s srčnim popuščanjem pojavijo prevodne motnje v srcu, zlasti levokračni blok. Posledica prevodnih motenj je nesinhrono krčenje levega prekata, zaradi česar se dodatno zmanjša iztisni delež in poslabša polnitev levega prekata, poslabša se tudi mitralna regurgitacija. Z dvoprekatno elektrostimulacijo srca (slika 2.98) zmanjšamo nesinhronost krčenja levega prekata. English:
Biventricular pacing is used to treat patients with heart failure in functional classes NYHA II to IV, with a left ventricular ejection fraction below 35% and a QRS complex width over 130 ms. Studies have shown that patients with left bundle branch block respond better to this treatment than patients with right bundle branch block. The treatment of heart failure with biventricular pacing is based on the understanding that in almost 30% of patients with heart failure, conduction disturbances in the heart develop, especially left bundle branch block. The consequence of these conduction disturbances is asynchronous contraction of the left ventricle, which further reduces the ejection fraction and impairs left ventricular filling, also worsening mitral regurgitation. Biventricular cardiac pacing (figure 2.98) reduces the asynchrony of left ventricular contraction.

Question:
25. Which conduction disturbances are not seen on a 12-lead ECG?
A. First-degree AV block
B. Third-degree AV block
C. Left posterior hemiblock
D. Left bundle branch block
E. First-degree sinoatrial block
Reference: Interna medicina (6. izd., str. XXXVIII, 1693). (2022). Medicinska fakulteta; Buča.
Context: Sinoatrijski blok. Podobno kakor pri atrioventrikularnih blokih tudi pri sinoatrijskem bloku razlikujemo blok prve, druge in tretje stopnje. Patofiziološko je sinoatrijski blok motnja sinoatrijskega prevajanja ali njegovo popolno prenehanje. Prvostopenjski sinoatrijski blok označuje trajno podaljšan čas sinoatrijskega prevajanja, brez izpadov; tega bloka ne moremo diagnosticirati elektrokardiografsko. English: Sinoatrial block. Just like with atrioventricular blocks, in sinoatrial block we distinguish between first, second, and third degrees. Pathophysiologically, sinoatrial block is a disturbance or complete cessation of sinoatrial conduction. First-degree sinoatrial block is characterized by a persistently prolonged conduction time of the sinoatrial impulse, without dropped beats; this block cannot be diagnosed by electrocardiography.

Question:
26. When a vascular stent is inserted into a peripheral artery, it is true that:
A. According to recommendations, single antiplatelet therapy with aspirin is sufficient
B. 1-3 months of combined antiplatelet therapy with aspirin and clopidogrel is recommended
C. Patients are prescribed anticoagulant therapy with warfarin
D. A combination of antiplatelet and anticoagulant therapy is recommended
E. Restenosis in peripheral artery stents does not occur
Text Answer: 1-3 months of combined antiplatelet therapy with aspirin and clopidogrel is recommended
Reference: Interna medicina (6. izd., str. XXXVIII, 1693). (2022). Medicinska fakulteta; Buča.
Context: Žilne opornice (stenti) dopolnjujejo perkutano transluminalno angioplastiko zlasti v področju medeničnih in podključničnih arterij. Pri postavitvi žilnih opornic v periferne arterije priporočamo dvojno antiagregacijsko zdravljenje z acetilsalicilno kislino in klopidogrelom v trajanju od 1 do 3 mesecev. English: Vascular stents (stents) complement percutaneous transluminal angioplasty, especially in the region of the pelvic and subclavian arteries. When vascular stents are placed in peripheral arteries, dual antiplatelet therapy with acetylsalicylic acid (aspirin) and clopidogrel is recommended for a duration of 1 to 3 months.

Question:
27. For patients with peripheral artery disease (PAD) who have symptoms of intermittent claudication, the first step to improve functional capacity is:
A. Immediate percutaneous transluminal angioplasty
B. Immediate surgery with arterial bypass
C. Interval walking training
D. Treatment with hemorheological drugs
E. Primary limb amputation
Reference: Interna medicina (6. izd., str. XXXVIII, 1693). (2022). Medicinska fakulteta; Buča.
Context: Intervalni trening hoje je osnova nefarmakološkega zdravljenja bolnikov z intermitentno klavdikacijo, ki lahko poveča sposobnost hoje za 50–200 odstotkov. Za doseganje optimalnega učinka je treba vaditi pod strokovnim nadzorom 35–50 minut 3- do 5-krat na teden vsaj šest mesecev. English: Interval walking training is the foundation of non-pharmacological treatment for patients with intermittent claudication, which can improve walking capacity by 50–200 percent. To achieve optimal results, it should be done under professional supervision for 35–50 minutes, 3 to 5 times per week for at least six months.

Question:
28. The most appropriate decision for stress testing in perfusion scintigraphy is:
A. Pharmacological stress testing for all patients, because it is the simplest and fastest
B. Dynamic stress testing (on a bicycle or treadmill) for all patients
C. For patients who cannot achieve sufficient dynamic stress, or who have a left bundle branch block or an implanted pacemaker, we perform pharmacological stress testing; others are stressed on a bicycle or treadmill
D. We do not even think about this at all
Text Answer: For patients who cannot achieve sufficient dynamic stress, or who have a left bundle branch block or an implanted pacemaker, we perform pharmacological stress testing; others are stressed on a bicycle or treadmill
Reference: Interna medicina (6. izd., str. XXXVIII, 1693). (2022). Medicinska fakulteta; Buča.
Context: Bolnike, ki niso sposobni telesne obremenitve, ki imajo kompletni levokračni blok ali pretežno elektrosistolnični ritem ob vgrajenem srčnem spodbujevalniku, »obremenimo« farmakološko z vazodilatatorji – agonisti adenozinskih receptorjev (dipiridamolom, adenozinom ali novejšim regadenozonom) ali pa z inotropnim sredstvom dobutaminom v kratkotrajni intravenski infuziji. English: Patients who are unable to undergo physical stress, who have a complete left bundle branch block, or who predominantly have an electrosystolic rhythm with an implanted cardiac pacemaker are "stressed" pharmacologically using vasodilators—adenosine receptor agonists (dipyridamole, adenosine, or the newer regadenoson), or with the inotropic agent dobutamine in a short-term intravenous infusion.

Question:
29. During reperfusion of the myocardium, in the dynamic changes of myoglobin or troponin concentration, we observe:
A. A late and low peak concentration
B. A rapid and high peak concentration
C. Uneven fluctuations in concentration
D. A continuous decrease in concentration
E. A continuous increase in concentration
Text Answer: A rapid and high peak concentration
Reference: Enzymatische Marker der Reperfusion bei akutem Myokardinfarkt. (1997). Herz, 22(3).
Context: Following reperfusion, creatine kinase (CK) and its isoform CK-MB, troponin, and myoglobin show an early and rapid rise to a high maximum value with rapid normalization.

Question:
30. CTA of the aorta (choose the INCORRECT statement):
A. It is the method of choice in acute aortic syndrome
B. It is less sensitive for detecting aortic dissection than classic aortic angiography
C. It enables classification of aortic dissections into Stanford type A and type B
D. It also allows visualization of the consequences or complications of aortic dissection, such as bowel ischemia or renal infarction
Text Answer: It is less sensitive for detecting aortic dissection than classic aortic angiography
Reference: Interna medicina (6. izd., str. XXXVIII, 1693). (2022). Medicinska fakulteta; Buča.
Context: Diagnoza. Anezrizmo trebušne aorte najpreprosteje prikažemo z ultrazvočno preiskavo trebuha. Računalniška tomografska angiografija prikaže aorto in veje aorte. Aortografija prikaže anevrizmo trebušne aorte manj zanesljivo, še posebno če jo delno izpolnjuje tromb, prikaže pa zelo zanesljivo prizadetost vej aorte. English: Diagnosis. An abdominal aortic aneurysm is most easily visualized by ultrasound examination of the abdomen. Computed tomographic angiography (CTA) displays the aorta and its branches. Aortography shows an abdominal aortic aneurysm less reliably, especially if it is partially filled with thrombus, but it does show involvement of the aortic branches very reliably.

Question:
31. A 60-year-old “natural healer” was found confused near the forest. On examination, he was restless, had hallucinations, dilated pupils, and red, dry skin. His ECG showed sinus tachycardia. What condition would you suspect?
A. Poisoning with deadly nightshade (Atropa belladonna)
B. Ethanol poisoning
C. Carbon monoxide poisoning
D. Hyperthyroidism
E. Snakebite from a venomous snake
Reference: Hanine Almubayedh et al. (2018). Clinical uses and toxicity of Atropa belladonna; an evidence-based comprehensive retrospective review (2003–2017). Bioscience Biotechnology Research Communications, 11(1)
Context: Major symptoms observed in these cases were related to Anticholinergic syndrome (ACS); tachycardia, dilated pupils, confusion, flushed dry skin, hallucinations and seizures in some cases along with a toxic megacolon.

Question:
32.Which of the following treatments would you choose for a patient with hypokalemia [K⁺ = 2.5 mmol/l] and episodes of non-sustained ventricular tachycardia?
A. Injection of calcium gluconate, infusion of insulin with glucose, oral administration of cation-exchange resin
B. Oral treatment, 40 mmol KCl, repeated as needed
C. Parenteral treatment, 10 mmol/h KCl, patient must be in the cardiology ward
D. Parenteral treatment, 20 mmol/h KCl, patient must be in the intensive care unit
E. Dialysis, patient must be in the intensive care unit
Text Answer: Parenteral treatment, 10 mmol/h KCl, patient must be in the cardiology ward
Reference: Interna medicina (6. izd., str. XXXVIII, 1693). (2022). Medicinska fakulteta; Buča.
Context: Hipokalemijo po možnosti vedno zdravimo vzročno. Sprejem v bolnišnico je potreben pri bolnikih s simptomatsko hipokalemijo, motnjami srčnega ritma in pri tistih, ki potrebujejo intravensko zdravljenje hipokalemije. Korekcija hipokalemije zahteva dodajanje kalijevih soli. Dajemo jih lahko oralno ali intravensko. Varneje je K⁺ dajati oralno, saj lahko hitre spremembe K⁺ v serumu, do katerih včasih pride pri intravenskem dajanju kalijevih soli, povzročijo motnje srčnega ritma ali prevajanja. English:Hypokalemia should always be treated causally whenever possible. Hospital admission is necessary for patients with symptomatic hypokalemia, arrhythmias, and for those requiring intravenous treatment of hypokalemia. Correction of hypokalemia requires supplementation with potassium salts, which can be administered orally or intravenously. It is safer to administer K⁺ orally because rapid changes in serum K⁺, which can occur with intravenous potassium supplementation, sometimes cause disturbances in heart rhythm or conduction.

Question:
33. A man with a known abdominal aortic aneurysm seeks medical help because for the past hour he has had severe pain in his right leg from the middle of the thigh downwards, accompanied by numbness. The leg appears obviously pale and cold. You feel a femoral pulse on the leg, but pulses lower down are absent. What is the diagnosis?
a. advanced peripheral arterial disease that does not require immediate intervention
b. rupture of the abdominal aortic aneurysm
c. thromboembolism in the right common iliac artery
d. thromboembolism in the right superficial femoral artery
e. iliofemoral deep vein thrombosis
Text Answer: thromboembolism in the right superficial femoral artery
Reference: Harrison’s Principles of Internal Medicine (2022)
Page: Chapter 302: Vascular Diseases of the Extremities
Context: Acute limb ischemia refers to a sudden decrease in limb perfusion that threatens the viability of the limb. It is usually caused by an embolus from the heart or proximal arteries, or by thrombosis of an atherosclerotic plaque. Symptoms include the ‘six P’s’: pain, pallor, pulselessness, paresthesia, paralysis, and poikilothermia. The presence of a known aneurysm (such as an abdominal aortic aneurysm) increases the risk of embolic events. Prompt diagnosis and revascularization are essential for limb salvage and to prevent major morbidity.

Question:
34. On an arteriographic image, which of the following is characteristic of Buerger's disease?
A. Large arteries of the pelvis and thigh are often affected
B. Along the arterial occlusion, we see spiral (corkscrew) collaterals that develop from the vasa vasorum and run in the vessel wall
C. Abundant atherosclerotic plaques in the arteries of the lower limbs
D. Digital arteries in the hands and feet are never affected
E. The angiographic image is the only criterion for diagnosing Buerger's disease
Text Answer: Along the arterial occlusion, we see spiral (corkscrew) collaterals that develop from the vasa vasorum and run in the vessel wall
Reference: Interna medicina (6. izd., str. XXXVIII, 1693). (2022). Medicinska fakulteta; Buča.
Context: Angiografsko dokažemo prizadetost malih in srednje velikih arterij na udih, kot so digitalne arterije prstov rok in nog, arterije palmarnega in plantarnega loka, radialna, ulnarna arterija in golenske arterije. Značilne so spiralaste, »vitičaste« kolaterale, ki se oblikujejo iz vasa vasorum in potekajo vzdolž žilne stene. Poplitealna arterija ostaja praviloma prehodna, prav tako brahialna arterija. English: Angiographically, involvement of small and medium-sized arteries in the limbs is demonstrated, such as the digital arteries of the fingers and toes, the arteries of the palmar and plantar arches, the radial and ulnar arteries, and the tibial arteries. Characteristic are spiral, “corkscrew” collaterals that develop from the vasa vasorum and run along the vessel wall.

Question:
35. What is the mortality of acute type A aortic dissection?
A. All patients die immediately
B. 10% per minute for the first 5 minutes, then 5% per hour
C. 10% per hour for the first 5 hours, then 5% per hour
D. 1-2% per hour for the first 48 hours
E. Patients rarely die from late complications
Text Answer: 1-2% per hour for the first 48 hours
Reference: Interna medicina (6. izd., str. XXXVIII, 1693). (2022). Medicinska fakulteta; Buča.
Context: Prognoza. Smrtnost disekcije aorte je velika, pri tipu A znaša brez zdravljenja 1 do 2 odstotka na uro prvih 48 ur. Pri operiranih bolnikih je pogosta ponovitev bolezni na drugem mestu aorte, ki lahko narekuje nov kirurški poseg, zato so indicirane kontrole, v prvem letu trikrat, nato vsako leto. English: Prognosis. The mortality of aortic dissection is high; in type A, it is 1 to 2 percent per hour during the first 48 hours if untreated. In operated patients, recurrence of the disease at another site of the aorta is common and may require a new surgical intervention; therefore, follow-up is indicated, three times in the first year, then annually.

Question:
36. The so-called Beck’s triad, characteristic of cardiac tamponade, means:
A. Arterial hypertension, bradycardia, mydriasis
B. Hypotension, decreased central venous pressure, loud first heart sound
C. Hypotension, increased central venous pressure, muffled heart sounds
D. Pulsus paradoxus, distended neck veins, third heart sound
E. Tachycardia, sweating, full neck veins
Text Answer: Hypotension, increased central venous pressure, muffled heart sounds
Reference: Interna medicina (6. izd., str. XXXVIII, 1693). (2022). Medicinska fakulteta; Buča.
Context: V klinični sliki tamponade izstopajo hipotenzija, zvišanje centralnega venskega tlaka in tiho srce (t. i. Beckova triada). Zaradi zmanjšanega minutnega volumna srca so prisotni še dispneja, ortopneja in refleksna tahikardija. English:In the clinical picture of tamponade, hypotension, increased central venous pressure, and muffled heart sounds stand out (the so-called Beck’s triad). Due to the reduced cardiac output, dyspnea, orthopnea, and reflex tachycardia are also present.

Question:
1. Scintigraphy with Tc-99m-labeled erythrocytes shows the source of bleeding when the bleeding rate is:
A. 0.02 ml/min
B. 0.05 ml/min
C. 0.04 ml/min
D. 0.03 ml/min
E. 0.01 ml/min
Reference: Harrison’s Principles of Internal Medicine (2022)
Context: Radionuclide-labeled RBC scintigraphy can detect bleeding rates as low as 0.04 mL/min.

Question:
2. For neuroendocrine tumors of the small intestine, it is true that:
A. They are benign tumors
B. The only treatment option is surgical
C. They are treated surgically, with chemotherapy, somatostatin analogs, and serotonin antagonists
D. They always secrete serotonin
E. They only occur in women
Text Answer: They are treated surgically, with chemotherapy, somatostatin analogs, and serotonin antagonists
Reference: Harrison’s Principles of Internal Medicine (2022)
Page: Chapter: 93 – Neoplasms of the Gastrointestinal Tract
Context: Neuroendocrine tumors of the gastrointestinal tract are managed by a multimodal approach that includes surgical resection for localized disease and medical therapies such as somatostatin analogs (e.g., octreotide), serotonin antagonists for carcinoid syndrome symptoms, and chemotherapy for advanced disease.

Question:
3. Radical resection of the colon (R0 resection) in colorectal cancer means:
A. removal of the colon segment with adequate safety margins in healthy tissue (no microscopic residual tumor, no macroscopic residual tumor) and removal of involved lymph nodes
B. limited resection of part of the colon without removal of regional lymph nodes
C. endoscopic resection of the tumor with a laser
D. endoscopic resection of the tumor with coagulation
E. enteroenteroanastomosis
Text Answer: removal of the colon segment with adequate safety margins in healthy tissue (no microscopic residual tumor, no macroscopic residual tumor) and removal of involved lymph nodes
Reference: Interna medicina (6. izd., str. XXXVIII, 1693). (2022). Medicinska fakulteta; Buča.
Context: Standardna radikalna resekcija (hemikolektomija) je monoblok resekcija ustreznega dela črevesa z zadostnim varnostnim robom z vsem pripadajočim mezenterijem in bezgavkami ter prekinitvijo pripadajočih žil na odcepiščih. Razširjena radikalna resekcija zajema praviloma še obe sosednji drenažni področji. Pri raku zgornje tretjine danke izvedemo sprednjo anteriorno resekcijo, pri raku srednje tretjine nizko sprednjo resekcijo z mezorektalno ekscizijo, izjemoma ekscizijo danke. English: Standard radical resection (hemicolectomy) is a monobloc resection of the appropriate part of the intestine with an adequate safety margin, including all associated mesentery and lymph nodes, as well as ligation of the corresponding vessels at their origins. Extended radical resection generally also includes the two adjacent drainage areas. In cancer of the upper third of the rectum, an anterior resection is performed; in cancer of the middle third, a low anterior resection with mesorectal excision is done, and in exceptional cases, excision of the rectum itself.

Question:
4. Truncal vagotomy in surgery for peptic ulcer disease means (mark the correct statement):
A. severing the vagal fibers to the body of the stomach while preserving those to the antrum
B. severing the branches of the vagus at the level of the cardia and performing a two-thirds resection of the stomach
C. severing both trunks of the vagus nerve at the level of the cardia
D. two-thirds resection of the stomach with Roux-en-Y anastomosis
E. two-thirds resection of the stomach
Text Answer: severing both trunks of the vagus nerve at the level of the cardia
Reference: Truncal vagotomy. (n.d.). In ScienceDirect Topics. Elsevier
Context: Truncal vagotomy is a surgical procedure that involves the division of both anterior and posterior vagal trunks at the esophageal hiatus, resulting in denervation of all vagal nerve-supplied viscera, and requiring a drainage procedure to address impaired gastric emptying.

Question:
5. A young female patient has abdominal pain accompanied by vomiting and tenesmus, fever, and bloody diarrhea. What is the likely diagnosis?
A. diverticulitis
B. Crohn's disease
C. ulcerative colitis
D. Hirschsprung's disease
E. acute appendicitis
Reference: Interna medicina (6. izd., str. XXXVIII, 1693). (2022). Medicinska fakulteta; Buča.
Context: Klinična slika in postavitev diagnoze. Na kronično vnetno črevesno bolezen posumimo pri bolniku s kronično drisko, ki je lahko krvava, s pridruženimi bolečinami v trebuhu, anemijo, malabsorpcijo. Pri vsaj tretjini bolnikov so prisotne tudi zunajčrevesne manifestacije bolezni, tj. prizadetost sklepov, kože in oči. Za postavitev diagnoze je potreben endoskopski pregled prebavil, pri katerem odvzamemo biopsije za patohistološko preiskavo, dodatno si pomagamo z laboratorijskimi in slikovnimi preiskavami (magnetresonančna enterografija, računalniška tomografija, ultrazvočni pregled črevesja). Diferencialno diagnozo prikazuje tabela 4.24. English: Clinical picture and establishing the diagnosis. Chronic inflammatory bowel disease is suspected in patients with chronic diarrhea, which may be bloody, accompanied by abdominal pain, anemia, and malabsorption. In at least one third of patients, extraintestinal manifestations of the disease are present, i.e., involvement of the joints, skin, and eyes. Establishing the diagnosis requires an endoscopic examination of the digestive tract, during which biopsies are taken for histopathological examination. Additional assistance comes from laboratory and imaging studies (magnetic resonance enterography, computed tomography, ultrasound of the bowel). The differential diagnosis is shown in Table 4.24.

Question:
6. In the clinical diagnosis of peritonitis, we find:
A. initially increased intestinal peristalsis, later paralytic ileus
B. increased cholesterol
C. hyperperistalsis of the intestine in the advanced form
D. metabolic alkalosis
E. polyuria
Text Answer: initially increased intestinal peristalsis, later paralytic ileus
Reference: Harrison’s Principles of Internal Medicine (2022)
Context: Peritonitis typically presents with abdominal pain, tenderness, guarding, and rigidity. Initially, there may be increased bowel sounds due to irritation, but as the condition progresses, intestinal motility decreases, leading to hypoactive or absent bowel sounds, indicative of paralytic ileus.

Question:
7. Among the indications for surgical treatment of ulcerative colitis, we do not include:
A. toxic megacolon
B. extraintestinal manifestations (arthritis, osteoporosis)
C. perforation
D. failure of medical treatment (no response to 5-aminosalicylic acid, corticosteroids, cyclosporine, and biologics)
E. bleeding
Reference: Interna medicina (6. izd., str. XXXVIII, 1693). (2022). Medicinska fakulteta; Buča.
Context: Operativno zdravljenje je indicirano pri življenjsko ogrožajočih zapletih, kot je toksični kolitis ali perforacija črevesa, ter pri lokalnih zapletih, kot so zožitve lumna črevesa, fistule in abscesi. Kirurško zdravimo tudi bolnike, pri katerih z zdravili ne uspemo obvladati vnetja. Pri medikamentoznem zdravljenju uporabljamo različne skupine zdravil. English: Surgical treatment is indicated in life-threatening complications, such as toxic colitis or bowel perforation, and in local complications such as bowel lumen strictures, fistulas, and abscesses. Surgery is also performed in patients in whom inflammation cannot be controlled with medication. Different groups of drugs are used in medical treatment.

Question:
8. What are precancerous changes of the gastric mucosa?
a) atrophic gastritis
b) intestinal metaplasia
c) adenomatous polyp
d) endocrine cell hyperplasia

A) a, b
B) a, b, c
C) a, c
D) b, c
E) a, b, c, d
Reference: Interna medicina (6. izd., str. XXXVIII, 1693). (2022). Medicinska fakulteta; Buča.
Context: 

Question:
9. Choose the correct statements that apply to cholecystolithiasis:
T1. It is mostly asymptomatic and is discovered incidentally.
T2. It can lead to colic with obstructive jaundice.
T3. It is diagnosed with ultrasound.
T4. Asymptomatic cholecystolithiasis is treated surgically.
T5. Courvoisier’s sign is characteristic.
a. T3, T4, T5
b. T2, T4, T5
c. T2, T3, T4
d. T1, T2, T3
Reference: Interna medicina (6. izd., str. XXXVIII, 1693). (2022). Medicinska fakulteta; Buča.
Context:
Klinična slika. Pogosto so asimptomatski. Za razliko od asimptomatske holecistolitiaze njen potek ni benignen, saj so pogosti življenjsko ogrožajoči zapleti. English: Clinical presentation. They are often asymptomatic. Unlike asymptomatic gallbladder stones, however, their course is not benign, because life-threatening complications are common.

Vodilni simptom holedocholitiaze je biliarna kolika. Bolniki bruhajo ali pa jih sili na bruhanje. Kadar kamen zamaši odtok žolča v dvanajstnik, se pojavi zlatenica, ob vnetju pa tudi vročina (holangitis). English: 
The leading symptom of choledocholithiasis is biliary colic. Patients vomit or experience an urge to vomit. When a stone obstructs bile flow into the duodenum, jaundice develops—and with inflammation, fever (cholangitis) also occurs.

S transabdominalnim ultrazvokom uspemo prikazati žolčne kamne v žolčevodu le pri 40 % bolnikov. Endoskopski ultrazvok (EUZ) ima v diagnostiki osrednjo vlogo zaradi visoke občutljivosti (95-odstotna) in specifičnosti (97-odstotna). English: With transabdominal ultrasound we can detect bile duct stones in only about 40 % of patients. Endoscopic ultrasound (EUS) plays a central diagnostic role because of its high sensitivity (95 %) and specificity (97 %).

Question:
10. Which of the following statements regarding gallbladder cancer is correct?
A. The risk of gallbladder cancer is increased in patients with a diffusely calcified gallbladder compared to those with a partially calcified gallbladder.
B. Screening for gallbladder cancer is recommended in patients with primary sclerosing cholangitis.
C. 50% of patients with gallbladder cancer do not have gallstones.
D. The risk of developing gallbladder cancer is higher with cholesterol stones than with pigment stones.
Text Answer: The risk of gallbladder cancer is increased in patients with a diffusely calcified gallbladder compared to those with a partially calcified gallbladder.
Reference: Interna medicina (6. izd., str. XXXVIII, 1693). (2022). Medicinska fakulteta; Buča.
Context: Etiopatogeneza. Vzrok nastanka raka na žolčniku je verjetno multifaktorski. Dejavniki tveganja so žolčni kamni, porcelanast žolčnik, adenomsk polipi žolčnika in primarni sklerozirajoči holangitis. Histološko gre pri veliki večini (80–95 odstotkov) bolnikov za adenokarcinom. Raste lokalno infiltrativno in hitro vrašča v sosednje organe in strukture. Zaseva limfogeno in hematogeno. English: Etiopathogenesis. The cause of gallbladder cancer is probably multifactorial. Risk factors include gallstones, porcelain gallbladder, adenomatous polyps of the gallbladder, and primary sclerosing cholangitis. Histologically, in the vast majority (80–95 percent) of patients, it is an adenocarcinoma. It grows locally infiltratively and rapidly invades adjacent organs and structures. It metastasizes via lymphatic and hematogenous routes.

Question:
11. Characteristics of a pancreatic pseudocyst include:
A. it is lined by epithelium of the pancreatic ducts  
B. it develops more often in biliary than in alcoholic pancreatitis  
C. it appears four or more weeks after disease onset  
D. all patients have symptoms attributable to the pseudocyst  
E. the pseudocyst is usually sterile  

Possible combinations:

A. A and C  
B. A and B  
C. C and E  
D. B and C  
E. A and E
Text Answer: it appears four or more weeks after disease onset
Reference: Interna medicina (6. izd., str. XXXVIII, 1693). (2022). Medicinska fakulteta; Buča.
Context:
Akutna peripankreatična tekočinska kolekcija in pankreatična psevdocista.
Do nastanka tekočinske kolekcije pride ponavadi v zgodnji fazi intersticijskega edematoznega pankreatitisa. Večina ostane kolekcija sterilna in se resorbira sama od sebe. Če kolekcija vztraja več kot štiri tedne, ima na CT s kontrastom dobro definirano steno in ne vsebuje solidnih vključkov, govorimo o pankreatični psevdocisti.
English: Acute peripancreatic fluid collection and pancreatic pseudocyst.
Fluid collections usually develop in the early phase of interstitial edematous pancreatitis. Most collections remain sterile and are reabsorbed spontaneously. If a collection persists for more than four weeks, demonstrates a well-defined wall on contrast-enhanced CT, and contains no solid components, it is termed a pancreatic pseudocyst.

Question:
12. A 32-year-old woman with obesity is being evaluated for retrosternal pain that has been present for the last three months. The pain worsens with food and at night. She currently smokes half a pack of cigarettes per day and drinks two glasses of wine a week. She is bothered by frequent belching. She denies weight loss, dysphagia, or hematemesis. Cardiovascular causes of the pain have been excluded. What is the best next step?
a. therapeutic trial with proton pump inhibitors (PPIs)
b. 24-hour esophageal pH monitoring
c. gastroscopy
d. contrast esophagography
e. esophageal manometry
Text Answer: therapeutic trial with proton pump inhibitors (PPIs)
Reference: Harrison’s Principles of Internal Medicine (2022)
Page: Chapter 346: Gastroesophageal Reflux Disease and Hiatal Hernia
Context: In patients with typical symptoms of GERD and no alarm features, an empirical trial of acid suppression with a proton pump inhibitor (PPI) is a reasonable initial approach. If symptoms improve, this supports the diagnosis of GERD.

Question:
13. How do we treat a patient with eosinophilic esophagitis?
A. With topically acting corticosteroids
B. With a proton-pump inhibitor
C. Surgical esophagectomy
D. With an elimination diet
E. All of the above are correct
Text Answer: All of the above are correct
Reference: Interna medicina (6. izd., str. XXXVIII, 1693). (2022). Medicinska fakulteta; Buča.
Context: Zdravljenje. Najučinkovitejše zdravljenje je preprečiti stik z alergenom v hrani, ki povzroča eozinofilni ezofagitis. Raziskave so odkrile šest skupin živil, ki jih postopoma ali po posebnih algoritmih izločamo iz bolnikove diete: mleko in mlečni izdelki, jajca, žita (predvsem z gluteno), soja, oreški in morska hrana. Najpogostejši sprožilci so v mleku in žitih, zato jih izločimo najprej. Izločevalna dieta je za bolnike pogosto težavna in se je ne držijo dosledno, zato po smernicah najprej uvedemo zdravljenje z zaviralci protonske črpalke. Okoli 40 odstotkov bolnikov se dobro odziva na tovrstno zdravljenje. V tem primeru govorimo o na zaviralce protonske črpalke odzivni ezofagalni eozinofiliji. Ta izraz se postopoma opušča, saj je vedno več dokazov, da gre le za obliko eozinofilnega ezofagitisa. Če zdravljenje z zaviralci protonske črpalke in dietni ukrepi niso učinkoviti, uvedemo zdravljenje z lokalnimi glukokortikoidi (flutikazon, budezonid, ciklezonid). Tako pride zdravilo v stik s sluznico požiralnika, pri čemer deluje lokalno in ima le minimalne sistemske učinke. Učinkovitost zdravljenja z lokalnimi glukokortikoidi ocenjujemo na podlagi biopsij, ki po uspešni terapiji prikazujejo zmanjšanje števila eozinofilcev. English: Treatment. The most effective treatment is to avoid the food allergen causing eosinophilic esophagitis. Studies have identified six food groups to be eliminated gradually or according to specific algorithms from the patient’s diet: milk and dairy products, eggs, grains (especially gluten-containing), soy, nuts, and seafood. The most common triggers are milk and grains, so these are eliminated first. An elimination diet is often difficult for patients to follow consistently, so guidelines recommend initiating proton-pump inhibitor therapy first. Approximately 40 percent of patients respond well to this treatment. In such cases, the condition is termed proton-pump inhibitor–responsive esophageal eosinophilia, a term that is being phased out as evidence increasingly supports that this is merely a variant of eosinophilic esophagitis. If proton-pump inhibitors and dietary measures are ineffective, treatment with topical glucocorticoids is introduced (fluticasone, budesonide, ciclesonide). This allows the medication to contact the esophageal mucosa directly, exerting a local effect with minimal systemic absorption. Treatment efficacy is assessed by biopsy, which, after successful therapy, shows a reduction in eosinophil count.

Question:
14. A 56-year-old patient with colorectal adenocarcinoma will certainly receive adjuvant chemotherapy after surgical resection if the pathology report identifies the disease as stage 3. Which of the following findings describes stage 3 colorectal adenocarcinoma?
a. tumor invades the muscularis propria, no lymph node involvement, no distant metastases
b. tumor invades the muscularis propria, no mention of lymph nodes
c. tumor invades the muscularis propria, lymph nodes are involved, no distant metastases
d. tumor limited to the mucosa, no lymph node involvement, no distant metastases
e. tumor invades the submucosa, lymph nodes are involved, no distant metastases
Text Answer: tumor invades the muscularis propria, lymph nodes are involved, no distant metastases
Reference: Interna medicina (6. izd., str. XXXVIII, 1693). (2022). Medicinska fakulteta; Buča.
Context: T1 pomeni, da tumor vrašča v submukozo; T2 pomeni, da tumor vrašča v mišične sloje (lamino muscularis proprio) črevesne stene, T3 pomeni, da tumor vrašča v perikolično perirektalno maščevje. T4 pomeni, da tumor prerašča steno črevesa, sega prek seroze in vrašča v sosednje organe. N0 pomeni, da ni zasevkov v regionalnih bezgavkah; N1 pomeni, da so zasevki v najmanj treh regionalnih bezgavkah; N2 pomeni, da so zasevki v več kot štirih regionalnih bezgavkah. M0 pomeni, da ni oddaljenih zasevkov; M1 pomeni oddaljene zasevke. English: T1 means that the tumor invades the submucosa; T2 means that the tumor invades the muscular layers (lamina muscularis propria) of the intestinal wall; T3 means that the tumor invades the pericolic or perirectal fat. T4 means that the tumor penetrates the intestinal wall, extends beyond the serosa, and invades adjacent organs. N0 means no metastases in regional lymph nodes; N1 means metastases in at least three regional lymph nodes; N2 means metastases in more than four regional lymph nodes. M0 means no distant metastases; M1 means distant metastases.

Question:
15. A 48-year-old woman with known celiac disease presents with persistent abdominal pain, bloating, and diarrhea. Her celiac diagnosis was made 5 years ago, at which time her EMA and tTG levels were elevated. She reports strict adherence to a gluten-free diet since diagnosis. Her weight is stable. Repeat serum tissue transglutaminase levels are elevated.
What is the most likely cause of her symptoms?
A. Non-celiac gluten sensitivity
B. Ulcerative jejunitis
C. Refractory celiac disease
D. Microscopic colitis
E. Gluten ingestion
Reference: Malamut, G., & Cellier, C. (2013). Refractory celiac disease. In Celiac Disease (Clinical Gastroenterology, Vol. 16, pp. 245–254). Springer.
Context: A small subset of celiac disease (CD) patients becomes refractory to a gluten-free diet with persistent symptoms of malabsorption and intestinal villous atrophy. Diagnosis of this condition, defined as refractory celiac disease (RCD), is made after exclusion of other small bowel diseases with villous atrophy.

Question:
16. A 62-year-old woman comes to the clinic because of diarrhea that started 3 days ago. She has also noticed blood in her stool twice; otherwise, she has been healthy. Seven days ago, she finished antibiotic treatment with amoxicillin and clavulanic acid for a dental root infection. What is the most likely diagnosis?
a. she probably has ulcerative colitis (UC), so she should be immediately referred for colonoscopy with biopsies
b. it is most likely hemorrhoidal bleeding, so she should be referred to a proctologist for hemorrhoid ligation
c. it is most likely infectious colitis, so prescribe ciprofloxacin and arrange a follow-up
d. it is antibiotic-associated diarrhea, so check for Clostridium difficile and its toxins A and B in stool
e. none of the above
Text Answer: it is antibiotic-associated diarrhea, so check for Clostridium difficile and its toxins A and B in stool
Reference: Harrison’s Principles of Internal Medicine (2022)
Context: Antibiotic-associated diarrhea is most commonly caused by Clostridium difficile. The diagnosis should be considered in any patient who develops diarrhea during or after antibiotic use. Laboratory testing for C. difficile toxins A and B in stool specimens is the standard diagnostic approach.

Question:
17. Which of the following statements about anal carcinoma is NOT true?
A. It is caused by HPV infection
B. It is usually first treated with chemoradiotherapy
C. Before starting treatment, it is necessary to delineate the extent of disease
D. The disease is diagnosed by clinical examination
E. It is an adenocarcinoma
Reference: Association of Coloproctology of Great Britain & Ireland. (2021, August 2). Anal cancer: ESMO Clinical Practice Guidelines for diagnosis, treatment and follow-up.
Context: Anal cancer is a rare disease that accounts for <1% and <3% of all new cancer diagnoses and gastrointestinal tumours, respectively. The most common histological subtype is squamous-cell carcinoma of the anus (SCCA) with an annual incidence of 0.5–2.0 in 100 000.

Question:
18. A 25-year-old man developed abdominal pain and frequent diarrhea, up to 5 times daily. He has not noticed blood in the stool. The diarrhea severely impacts him because he has to have bowel movements twice at night. After defecation, the abdominal pain subsides. He also has urgent urges to defecate, which strongly interferes with his daily activities. After three months, he visited a doctor and reported, in addition to the abdominal pain, marked fatigue and a weight loss from 85 to 81 kg over three months. On clinical examination, the abdomen was slightly tender in the ileocecal area, but no clear palpable mass was found. To differentiate between functional disorders and organic disease, in this age group, the most appropriate sequence of investigations is:
a. CT, if findings are positive, then colonoscopy
b. colonoscopy, if findings are positive, then fecal calprotectin to confirm the diagnosis
c. fecal calprotectin, if negative, then colonoscopy
d. fecal calprotectin, if positive, then colonoscopy
e. colonoscopy, if findings are positive, then CT
Text Answer: fecal calprotectin, if positive, then colonoscopy
Reference: Interna medicina (6. izd., str. XXXVIII, 1693). (2022). Medicinska fakulteta; Buča.
Context: Poleg standardnih krvnih parametrov, kot so krvna slika, C-reaktivni protein (CRP) in sedimentacija, uporabljamo še novejši parameter, fekalni kalprotektin. Kalprotektin se nahaja v nevtrofilnih granulocitih in se iz vnete črevesne sluznice sprošča v lumen prebavne cevi. Je občutljiv biološki označevalec vnetja črevesne sluznice in že uveljavljen presejalni test za razlikovanje med organskimi in funkcionalnimi vzroki črevesne simptomatike. Koncentracija kalprotektina v blatu dobro korelira z endoskopsko aktivnostjo bolezni pri Crohnovi bolezni in ulceroznem kolitisu. English: In addition to standard blood parameters, such as blood count, C-reactive protein (CRP), and sedimentation, we also use a newer parameter, fecal calprotectin. Calprotectin is found in neutrophil granulocytes and is released from the inflamed intestinal mucosa into the lumen of the digestive tract. It is a sensitive biological marker of intestinal mucosal inflammation and is already an established screening test to differentiate between organic and functional causes of intestinal symptoms. The concentration of calprotectin in the stool correlates well with endoscopic disease activity in Crohn's disease and ulcerative colitis.

Question:
19. Najpomembnejša značilnost ascitesa pri spontanem bakterijskem peritonitisu je (obkroži pravilni odgovor):
a. Je gost, zelen in značilnega vonja.
b. Koncentracija LDH je višja od serumske.
c. Povišano število nevtrofilnih granulocitov.
d. Povišana koncentracija proteinov v ascitesu.
e. V ascitesu je koncentracija glukoze vsaj dvakrat nižja od serumske.
Reference: Interna medicina (6. izd., str. XXXVIII, 1693). (2022). Medicinska fakulteta; Buča.
Context:
Diagnozo potrdimo z laboratorijsko preiskavo punktata ascitesa. Pri spontanem bakterijskem peritonitisu je ascites navadno moten, ima značilnosti transudata, koncentracija beljakovin v ascitesu pa je ponavadi nizka, pod 10 g/L. Ključno za postavitev diagnoze je število nevtrofilcev v mm³ ascitesa. Če je število nevtrofilnih levkocitov večje ali enako 250, gre za spontani bakterijski peritonitis, ne glede na to, ali so klinični simptomi in znaki okužbe navzoči ali ne, in ne glede na to, ali je kultura ascitesa pozitivna ali negativna. English:
The diagnosis is confirmed by laboratory examination of the ascitic fluid. In spontaneous bacterial peritonitis, the ascitic fluid is usually turbid, has the characteristics of a transudate, and the protein concentration in the ascitic fluid is usually low, below 10 g/L. The key to establishing the diagnosis is the number of neutrophils per mm³ in the ascitic fluid. If the number of neutrophilic leukocytes is greater than or equal to 250, it is considered spontaneous bacterial peritonitis, regardless of whether clinical symptoms and signs of infection are present or not, and regardless of whether the culture of the ascitic fluid is positive or negative.

Question:
20. Acute pancreatitis (circle the correct answers, multiple correct):
a) Ranson’s criteria
b) Glasgow criteria
c) APACHE
d) Child-Turcotte-Pugh classification

A) a, b
B) a, b, c
C) a, c
D) b, c
E) c, d
Reference: Prasanna, G., et al. (2020). Scoring Systems to Predict the Severity of Acute Pancreatitis—A Review. International Journal of Surgery & Surgical Techniques, 4(1), Article 000140.
Context:
“There are several scoring systems to assess the severity of AP. The few to be named are:
Bedside index of severity in acute pancreatitis (BISAP)
Acute physiology and chronic health evaluation–II (APACHE-II)
Harmless acute pancreatitis score (HAPS)
Multiple organ system score (MOSS)
Ranson scores
Glasgow scores
Balthazar computer tomography severity index (BCTSI) scores
Panc 3 Score”

Question:
21. Systemic inflammatory response syndrome (SIRS) is a syndrome of the early phase of acute pancreatitis and is a predictive factor for: (Which answer is NOT correct?)
A. Higher risk of local complications
B. Higher risk of dysfunction of one or more organ systems
C. Higher risk of infection of necrosis in the first 48 hours
D. Higher risk of severe acute pancreatitis
E. Higher risk of mortality
Text Answer: Higher risk of infection of necrosis in the first 48 hours
Reference: Harrison’s Principles of Internal Medicine (2022)
Context: The presence of SIRS at admission or within the first 48 hours is associated with an increased risk of organ failure and mortality. However, early SIRS is not a reliable predictor of infected pancreatic necrosis.

Question:
22. Jaundice becomes clinically visible at a bilirubin concentration that:
A. exceeds the upper limit of normal
B. exceeds the upper limit of normal by 50%
C. exceeds the upper limit of normal by 100%
D. exceeds the upper limit of normal by 300%
E. can also be within the normal range
Text Answer: exceeds the upper limit of normal by 300%
Reference: Interna medicina (6. izd., str. XXXVIII, 1693). (2022). Medicinska fakulteta; Buča.
Context: Opredelitev. Zlatenica je rumeno obarvanje kože in sluznic zaradi povišane serumske koncentracije vsega bilirubina. Normalna vrednost vsega bilirubina pri odraslih je do 17 µmol/l, zlatenica postane vidna pri 50 µmol/l, najprej na beločnicah in pod jezikom. English: Definition. Jaundice is the yellow discoloration of the skin and mucous membranes due to an elevated serum concentration of total bilirubin. The normal value of total bilirubin in adults is up to 17 µmol/l; jaundice becomes visible at 50 µmol/l, first in the sclera and under the tongue.

Question:
1. Which statements are correct for lymphomas?
A. They are more common than acute leukemias
B. The stage is determined using the Duke classification
C. Non-Hodgkin lymphomas are more common than Hodgkin's
D. At diagnosis, some patients do not require treatment

A. Correct answers are: A, B, D
B. Correct answers are: A, C, D
C. Correct answers are: A, B, C, D
D. Correct answers are: B, C, D
Text Answer: The stage is determined using the Duke classification
Reference: Strojan, P., & Hočevar, M. (Eds.). (2018). Onkologija: Učbenik za študente medicine [Oncology: Textbook for medical students] (1st ed.). Onkološki inštitut Ljubljana.
Context:
The overall incidence of lymphoid neoplasms is 12.1/100,000, placing this group in seventh place among all cancers. Incidence varies significantly across different regions of the world—higher incidence rates are characteristic of industrialized, developed countries, while the actual burden of disease in developing countries cannot be precisely assessed. The lowest incidence rates have been found in East Asia.

The frequency of non-Hodgkin lymphomas has been increasing year by year, but in some countries (e.g., the USA), the rise in incidence has already plateaued. In the period from 1975 to 1991 in the USA, the incidence increased by 3.6% annually, while from 1991 to 2003, it increased by 0.4% annually. In Slovenia, the incidence of non-Hodgkin lymphomas in the period 2006 to 2010 was 9.8% higher than in the period 2001 to 2005; an 8.7% increase in incidence was expected in 2013 compared to the period 2006 to 2010.

The incidence of Hodgkin lymphoma has remained roughly the same for a long time. Hodgkin lymphoma most commonly affects young adults aged 15 to 34 and older adults over the age of 50. The incidence of non-Hodgkin lymphomas increases exponentially with age between 20 and 79 years.

In Slovenia, more than 650 patients with newly diagnosed lymphoid neoplasms are treated annually. According to the Cancer Registry of the Republic of Slovenia, in 2012, 614 patients were diagnosed with B and T cell lymphomas (excluding acute lymphoblastic leukemias, which affected 22 patients in the same year), and 34 patients were diagnosed with Hodgkin lymphoma.

Question:
2. What are two main late complications of anthracycline chemotherapy (e.g., doxorubicin)?
a. Hepatic cirrhosis and renal failure
b. Pulmonary fibrosis and neuropathy
c. Cardiomyopathy and secondary leukemia
d. Osteoporosis and diabetes mellitus
e. Thrombosis and stroke
Reference: Strojan, P., & Hočevar, M. (Eds.). (2018). Onkologija: Učbenik za študente medicine [Oncology: Textbook for medical students] (1st ed.). Onkološki inštitut Ljubljana.
Context:
Patients treated for lymphomas have an increased risk of developing acute leukemia (most often acute myeloid leukemia) or myelodysplastic syndrome, which is a consequence of previous treatment with alkylating agents, etoposide, or doxorubicin.
The most common cause of cardiac complications in cancer patients are anthracyclines. They play a key role in the treatment of many types of cancer, both in adults and in children, and are among the most effective anticancer drugs, having been in use for over forty years. The main representative of this group is doxorubicin. They can cause both acute and chronic cardiotoxicity. Acute toxicity, which presents as transient heart failure or arrhythmia, is usually reversible, whereas chronic toxicity is typically irreversible. The latter results from the loss of myofibrils and damage to the cardiomyocytes in the heart muscle. Over one or more years—or even decades—after treatment, this can lead to asymptomatic systolic and/or diastolic dysfunction of the left ventricle, and subsequently to congestive cardiomyopathy and congestive heart failure.

Question:
3. According to WHO 1973, how are urothelial carcinoma differentiation grades defined?
a. G1 – poorly differentiated; G2 – moderately differentiated; G3 – well differentiated
b. G1 – well differentiated; G2 – moderately differentiated; G3 – poorly differentiated
c. G1 – low mitotic index; G2 – moderate mitotic index; G3 – high mitotic index
d. G1 – superficially invasive; G2 – muscle-invasive; G3 – metastasized
e. G1 – papillary; G2 – nodular; G3 – flat
Text Answer: G1 – well differentiated; G2 – moderately differentiated; G3 – poorly differentiated
Reference: Strojan, P., & Hočevar, M. (Eds.). (2018). Onkologija: Učbenik za študente medicine [Oncology: Textbook for medical students] (1st ed.). Onkološki inštitut Ljubljana.
Context:
Transitional cell carcinomas are classified according to the degree of differentiation using two classification systems, both of which are in use. According to the 1973 WHO classification, they are divided into:
G1 – well-differentiated
G2 – moderately differentiated
G3 – poorly differentiated

According to the 1998 WHO classification, they are divided into:
papillary urothelial neoplasm of low malignant potential
low-grade urothelial carcinoma
high-grade urothelial carcinoma
